title: feat: autenticação e entitlement únicos do Simplicio
body:
## Objetivo
Implementar a identidade única do Simplicio Code com device login, sessão renovável e entitlement validado pelo backend Simplicio. O cliente não deve oferecer BYOK nem armazenar credenciais de provedores.

## Passo a passo
1. Definir endpoints device/authorize, device/token, refresh, revoke e entitlement.
2. Exibir código e URL de ativação no TUI, headless e ACP.
3. Guardar refresh token no Keychain macOS, Secret Service Linux e Credential Manager Windows.
4. Manter access token apenas em memória e renová-lo com rotação.
5. Bloquear inferência fail-closed quando entitlement estiver ausente, expirado ou revogado.
6. Implementar logout local e revogação remota.
7. Migrar sessões legadas na primeira atualização compatível.
8. Documentar estados de autenticação e mensagens de recuperação.

## Critérios de aceite
- Nenhuma chave upstream existe no cliente, config, log ou telemetria.
- Login funciona nos três sistemas suportados e em terminal sem browser.
- Tokens expirados e revogados não autorizam novas requisições.
- Refresh token rotacionado invalida o anterior.
- Logout remove segredo local e encerra sessão remota.
- TUI, headless e ACP apresentam estados equivalentes.
- A API de entitlement retorna plano, limites e validade sem revelar provedor interno.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Unitários: máquina de estados, expiração, clock skew, refresh e logout.
- Integração: device flow completo com backend fake e keychain por plataforma.
- Segurança: replay, token roubado, revogação, redaction de logs e permissões do storage.
- Sistema: login, reinício do app, inferência, expiração, renovação e logout.
- Regressão: usuários beta sem login recebem migração explícita, nunca fallback BYOK.
- Cobertura mínima: 85% nos módulos novos de auth.

title: feat: gateway privado único de inferência
body:
## Objetivo
Criar o gateway privado único do Simplicio Code. O aplicativo fala apenas com domínios Simplicio; OpenRouter e qualquer provedor futuro permanecem exclusivamente no servidor.

## Contrato inicial
- GET /v1/code/models expõe apenas Simplicio-1.
- POST /v1/code/chat/completions aceita streaming OpenAI-compatible.
- GET /v1/code/usage devolve limites agregados da assinatura.
- Cabeçalho Authorization recebe somente token Simplicio.
- Roteamento inicial interno: tencent/hy3:free no OpenRouter.

## Passo a passo
1. Definir schemas versionados, erros e idempotência.
2. Implementar proxy streaming com cancelamento e backpressure.
3. Resolver segredo OpenRouter no secret manager do servidor.
4. Mapear Simplicio-1 para o modelo interno sem expor o slug upstream.
5. Aplicar entitlement, rate limit, orçamento por usuário e circuit breaker.
6. Adicionar timeout, retry seguro e fallback controlado por configuração.
7. Redigir prompts e payloads em logs; manter auditoria de metadados.
8. Publicar OpenAPI e ambiente staging.
9. Trocar o cliente beta do OpenRouter direto para o gateway.

## Critérios de aceite
- Nenhuma credencial ou hostname upstream aparece no binário distribuído.
- Simplicio-1 é o único nome público inicial.
- Streaming, cancelamento e tool calls funcionam ponta a ponta.
- Limites são aplicados por usuário e por assinatura.
- Falha do upstream produz erro Simplicio estável e rastreável.
- O segredo pode ser rotacionado sem nova versão do cliente.
- Auditoria não armazena prompt, código ou resposta por padrão.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Contrato OpenAPI e golden payloads.
- Integração com mock OpenRouter: stream, tool calls, 401, 429, 5xx e timeout.
- Carga: concorrência, backpressure e orçamento.
- Segurança: SSRF, header injection, secret scanning e isolamento entre tenants.
- Sistema: cliente real em staging usando somente domínio Simplicio.
- Cobertura mínima: 85% do gateway e 100% das regras de entitlement.

title: feat: mover busca, edição e execução para o Runtime
body:
## Objetivo
Completar a fusão com o Simplicio Runtime. Toda leitura, busca, edição e execução sobre o projeto deve atravessar ferramentas MCP versionadas, com sandbox e action gate. Não pode existir fallback local silencioso.

## Passo a passo
1. Inventariar cada acesso a filesystem e processo em TUI, headless, workspace e ACP.
2. Definir contratos MCP para search, list, stat, edit, write, delete e exec.
3. Implementar adaptadores Runtime no mesmo ponto central usado pelo filesystem atual.
4. Migrar busca textual e glob.
5. Migrar apply patch, write e delete com transação e rollback.
6. Migrar comandos de terminal com política, cwd e limites explícitos.
7. Remover ou bloquear backends locais restantes.
8. Adicionar capability negotiation e erro de versão incompatível.
9. Registrar métricas agregadas de latência e economia sem conteúdo do usuário.

## Critérios de aceite
- 100% dos caminhos de leitura, busca, mutação e execução usam Runtime MCP.
- Runtime ausente ou incompatível bloqueia a operação com instrução clara.
- Sandbox impede path traversal, symlink escape e execução fora da política.
- Escritas são atômicas e auditáveis; falhas não deixam arquivo parcial.
- TUI, headless, workspace e ACP usam o mesmo contrato.
- Nenhum teste depende de fallback local não declarado.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Unitários de path normalization, capabilities e tradução de erros.
- Integração com servidor MCP real e fake.
- Segurança: traversal, symlink race, command injection, arquivos grandes e binários.
- Sistema nas quatro superfícies.
- Regressão de leitura fail-closed já entregue.
- Benchmark de leitura, busca, patch e comando contra baseline.
- Cobertura mínima: 85% dos adaptadores Runtime.

title: feat: tornar o Mapper automático uma fonte de contexto incremental
body:
## Objetivo
Transformar o mapa iniciado ao abrir a pasta em contexto incremental, observável e reutilizável pelo agente.

## Passo a passo
1. Versionar o contrato simplicio.map-result.
2. Persistir cache por hash do repositório e versão do Runtime.
3. Expor estado: aguardando, mapeando, pronto, degradado e falhou.
4. Injetar resumo estrutural no contexto do Simplicio-1 com orçamento fixo.
5. Atualizar apenas arquivos alterados por watcher.
6. Permitir cancelar, reindexar e diagnosticar pelo TUI e headless.
7. Invalidar cache em troca de branch, worktree ou versão de schema.

## Critérios de aceite
- Abrir pasta dispara um único mapa sem bloquear a UI.
- O agente recebe visão geral antes de planejar mudanças, respeitando orçamento.
- Reabertura quente reutiliza cache válido.
- Mudanças incrementais aparecem sem reindexação total.
- Falha do mapa não permite leitura direta fora do Runtime.
- Estado e duração ficam visíveis sem expor caminhos sensíveis em telemetria.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Unitários de deduplicação, invalidação e orçamento.
- Integração com repositório pequeno, monorepo e worktree.
- Sistema: selecionar pasta, mapear, perguntar arquitetura e alterar branch.
- Concorrência: duas sessões na mesma pasta.
- Benchmark frio, quente e incremental.
- Cobertura mínima: 85% do coordenador de mapeamento.

title: feat: concluir identidade visual Simplicio Brasil e auditoria de marca
body:
## Objetivo
Remover referências públicas à marca anterior e consolidar Simplicio Code com tema verde e amarelo acessível.

## Passo a passo
1. Inventariar textos, screenshots, docs, binários, eventos, URLs e assets.
2. Renomear todas as superfícies públicas para Simplicio Code.
3. Preservar identificadores internos somente quando necessários à compatibilidade e documentá-los.
4. Finalizar tema Simplicio Brasil dark e light.
5. Criar logo, ícone, splash, paleta e tokens semânticos.
6. Atualizar help, welcome, erros, updater, manpages e documentação.
7. Revisar licenças, notices e atribuição do fork.

## Critérios de aceite
- Busca automatizada não encontra Grok Build em superfícies públicas.
- O binário e todos os modos exibem Simplicio Code.
- Tema padrão usa verde e amarelo sem prejudicar erro, sucesso e contraste.
- Contraste atende WCAG AA onde aplicável.
- Compatibilidade interna remanescente tem justificativa em documento.
- THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES e licença estão corretos.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Snapshot/golden das telas principais.
- Scanner de strings no binário e artefatos.
- Testes de aliases de tema e migração de configuração.
- Auditoria de contraste e daltonismo.
- Smoke em terminal truecolor, 256 e 16 cores.

title: feat: pipeline de release beta, instaladores e atualização assinada
body:
## Objetivo
Publicar 0.3.0-beta.1 de forma reproduzível e criar atualização segura para macOS, Linux e Windows.

## Passo a passo
1. Definir matriz de targets e política beta/stable.
2. Gerar binários reproduzíveis e SBOM.
3. Assinar artefatos e checksums.
4. Criar instaladores shell, Homebrew, PowerShell e pacote Windows.
5. Apontar updater somente para releases do simplicio-code.
6. Implementar manifest versionado, rollout gradual e rollback.
7. Marcar prerelease no GitHub e publicar notas de limitação.
8. Validar instalação limpa, update e downgrade seguro.

## Critérios de aceite
- Tag e versão são 0.3.0-beta.1.
- Artefatos de todos os targets têm checksum, assinatura e SBOM.
- Updater rejeita manifest ou binário adulterado.
- Canal beta nunca atualiza automaticamente para origem anterior.
- Instalação não exige toolchain Rust.
- Rollback preserva sessão e configuração compatível.
- Release notes informam Runtime obrigatório e status do login.

## Testes obrigatórios
- CI em macOS, Ubuntu e Windows.
- Instalação limpa e atualização N-1 para beta.
- Assinatura inválida, download truncado e interrupção de energia simulada.
- Smoke simplicio-code --version e abertura de projeto.
- Teste de rollback e migração de configuração.

title: security: eliminar segredos do cliente e estabelecer rotação e scanning
body:
## Objetivo
Garantir que nenhuma credencial OpenRouter ou de outro provedor seja distribuída, registrada ou persistida pelo Simplicio Code.

## Passo a passo
1. Revogar e rotacionar toda chave compartilhada em chat ou histórico.
2. Executar secret scan no histórico Git e artefatos.
3. Adicionar gitleaks ou equivalente no pre-commit e CI.
4. Centralizar segredos no secret manager do gateway.
5. Implementar redaction estruturada de headers, URLs e erros.
6. Gerar inventário de superfícies de log, crash report e telemetria.
7. Criar runbook de incidente e rotação.
8. Bloquear release quando scanner encontrar segredo.

## Critérios de aceite
- Nenhuma chave upstream está em source, histórico alcançável, release ou cliente.
- A chave comprometida está revogada e evidenciada fora da issue.
- Rotação no servidor não exige atualização do app.
- Logs e traces nunca incluem Authorization ou payload sensível.
- CI falha em fixtures de segredo realistas.
- Runbook define detecção, revogação, comunicação e recuperação.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Secret scan completo e em diff.
- Testes unitários de redaction.
- Canary secrets em logs, telemetry e crash dumps.
- Inspeção strings do binário e pacote.
- Teste de rotação sob tráfego.

title: feat: sincronizar login e entitlement durante updates de versões legadas
body:
## Objetivo
Migrar usuários de versões beta/legadas para o login Simplicio durante uma atualização futura, sem perda de trabalho e sem chave manual.

## Passo a passo
1. Catalogar formatos e versões de config e sessão.
2. Definir manifest de migração idempotente e reversível.
3. Detectar primeira execução pós-update.
4. Preservar backup criptografado das configurações compatíveis.
5. Solicitar device login e validar entitlement.
6. Remover configuração de provedor apenas após confirmação.
7. Retomar sessões com alias Simplicio-1.
8. Telemetria agregada de sucesso e códigos de falha.
9. Oferecer rollback que não restaure segredos obsoletos.

## Critérios de aceite
- Migração roda uma vez e pode ser retomada após interrupção.
- Nenhum projeto, histórico ou configuração suportada é perdido.
- Usuário não vê OpenRouter nem modelo interno.
- Falha de login mantém dados e apresenta recuperação.
- Rollback é seguro e documentado.
- Migrações N-2, N-1 e beta atual são suportadas.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Fixtures de todas as versões suportadas.
- Interrupção em cada etapa e retomada.
- Sessão expirada, conta sem entitlement e dispositivo offline.
- Upgrade, downgrade e upgrade repetido.
- Testes de privacidade do backup e logs.

title: ci: matriz completa de qualidade, segurança e compatibilidade
body:
## Objetivo
Criar um gate obrigatório de qualidade para que nenhuma release seja emitida sem evidência reproduzível.

## Passo a passo
1. Adicionar fmt, clippy com warnings negados e cargo check focado.
2. Executar unitários e integrações por crate.
3. Subir Simplicio Runtime real para testes MCP.
4. Rodar e2e TUI por PTY, headless e ACP.
5. Adicionar cargo-deny, audit, licenses e secret scanning.
6. Cobertura com relatório e threshold.
7. Cache com limite e limpeza para evitar disco cheio.
8. Matriz macOS, Linux e Windows.
9. Publicar evidências e bloquear merge/release.

## Critérios de aceite
- Branch protection exige todos os checks.
- Cobertura nova mínima de 85% nos módulos Simplicio.
- Runtime indisponível testa comportamento fail-closed.
- CI não depende de segredo de fork em PR não confiável.
- Cache tem quota e política de expiração.
- Falhas produzem logs úteis sem segredos.
- Release workflow reutiliza exatamente os gates de PR.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Meta-testes que provam falha do pipeline em fmt, lint, teste, segredo e licença.
- Matriz dos três sistemas.
- E2E das quatro superfícies de leitura.
- Teste de artefato final, não apenas código fonte.

title: perf: medir e garantir economia de tokens e latência do Runtime
body:
## Objetivo
Demonstrar quantitativamente que Runtime, Mapper e modo conciso reduzem tokens sem reduzir correção.

## Passo a passo
1. Definir corpus versionado de repositórios e tarefas.
2. Criar baseline do fork sem mapa/context compression.
3. Medir tokens de entrada, saída, cache, tool output, latência e sucesso.
4. Comparar leitura direta equivalente, Runtime frio, quente e incremental.
5. Criar orçamento por operação e truncamento semântico.
6. Adicionar benchmark contínuo com limites de regressão.
7. Publicar metodologia e resultados reproduzíveis.

## Critérios de aceite
- Métrica primária combina correção primeiro e tokens depois.
- Resultados incluem p50, p95, variância e tamanho do repo.
- Nenhum ganho é aceito se reduzir taxa de sucesso além do limite definido.
- Regressão maior que 10% em tokens ou latência bloqueia release, salvo waiver.
- Dados não contêm código privado nem prompts de usuários.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Golden tasks com resposta verificável.
- Benchmark macro e micro.
- Repetições suficientes para intervalo de confiança.
- Teste de arquivos grandes, monorepo e mapa inválido.
- Comparação fria, quente e incremental.

title: privacy: telemetria mínima e controles de dados do Simplicio Code
body:
## Objetivo
Definir telemetria first-party mínima, opt-out e sem código do usuário, removendo destinos herdados incompatíveis.

## Passo a passo
1. Inventariar eventos, crash reports, traces e endpoints existentes.
2. Desabilitar destinos herdados por padrão.
3. Definir schema allowlist com finalidade e retenção.
4. Excluir prompts, respostas, conteúdo, nomes de arquivos e caminhos completos.
5. Implementar opt-out e comando de diagnóstico.
6. Aplicar pseudonimização e rotação de identificadores.
7. Documentar política e fluxo de exclusão.
8. Testar proxy que captura toda saída de rede.

## Critérios de aceite
- Cliente contata apenas domínios explicitamente documentados.
- Nenhum conteúdo de projeto sai por telemetria.
- Opt-out é respeitado antes do primeiro evento.
- Usuário consegue listar o que seria enviado.
- Retenção e exclusão são aplicadas no backend.
- Crash reports passam por redaction.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Network allowlist em e2e.
- Fixtures com segredos e paths canário.
- Opt-out desde primeira execução.
- Inspeção de crash report.
- Testes de schema rejeitando campos desconhecidos.

title: docs: onboarding beta, arquitetura e runbooks operacionais
body:
## Objetivo
Entregar documentação suficiente para instalar, operar, depurar e contribuir com o beta sem conhecimento do fork original.

## Passo a passo
1. Reescrever quickstart em português e inglês.
2. Documentar dependência e instalação do Simplicio Runtime.
3. Explicar fluxo Mapper, leitura MCP e fail-closed.
4. Documentar Simplicio-1 sem revelar credenciais.
5. Criar troubleshooting de login, Runtime, mapa, rede e updater.
6. Criar arquitetura, threat model e decisões ADR.
7. Criar runbooks de release, rollback, rotação e incidente.
8. Remover links obsoletos e validar todos os comandos.

## Critérios de aceite
- Usuário novo instala e abre um projeto seguindo apenas o quickstart.
- Documentação não pede chave OpenRouter.
- Todos os links e comandos são verificados em CI.
- Limitações beta são explícitas.
- Diagramas refletem cliente, Runtime, gateway e site Simplicio.
- Runbooks têm dono, pré-condições e rollback.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Doc tests e link checker.
- Teste de onboarding em ambiente limpo.
- Revisão de segurança das instruções.
- Validação de comandos nos três sistemas.

title: feat: integrar site_simpleti como backend de conta e assinatura do Simplicio Code
body:
## Objetivo
Conectar o produto ao backend do site_simpleti para device login, assinatura, entitlement e gestão de dispositivos, mantendo o repositório simplicio-code desacoplado por contrato.

## Passo a passo
1. Localizar o módulo correto no site_simpleti e definir ownership.
2. Criar APIs versionadas de device flow, entitlement, revoke e usage.
3. Modelar produto Simplicio Code, planos, limites e estados de assinatura.
4. Implementar tela web de autorização de dispositivo.
5. Criar gestão de dispositivos e revogação pelo usuário.
6. Integrar webhooks do provedor de pagamento de forma idempotente.
7. Publicar OpenAPI consumida pelo cliente e gateway.
8. Configurar staging, observabilidade e suporte.
9. Abrir issues espelho no site_simpleti com links cruzados.

## Critérios de aceite
- Contrato cliente-site é versionado e testado.
- Assinatura ativa concede entitlement; cancelada ou vencida revoga conforme política.
- Device codes são curtos, expiram e são single-use.
- Usuário visualiza e revoga dispositivos.
- Webhooks repetidos não duplicam estado.
- Dados de conta não se misturam com prompts ou código.
- Issue de implementação correspondente existe no site_simpleti.

## Testes obrigatórios
- Contrato entre repos.
- Device flow e entitlement ponta a ponta.
- Webhooks duplicados, fora de ordem e inválidos.
- Segurança de CSRF, session fixation, brute force e tenant isolation.
- E2E cliente, browser, site e gateway em staging.

title: epic: Simplicio Code beta até 1.0
body:
## Objetivo\nConduzir o Simplicio Code do beta Runtime-first até 1.0 com assinatura própria, gateway privado e distribuição segura.\n\n## Fase 1 - Beta 0.3\n- [ ] #6 Mapper incremental e contexto\n- [ ] #7 identidade Simplicio Brasil\n- [ ] #8 release, instaladores e updater\n- [ ] #9 segurança de segredos\n- [ ] #11 CI e gates\n- [ ] #12 benchmark de tokens\n- [ ] #14 documentação e runbooks\n\n## Fase 2 - Produto 1.0\n- [ ] #3 login e entitlement\n- [ ] #4 gateway privado Simplicio-1\n- [ ] #5 Runtime para todas as operações\n- [ ] #10 migração durante updates\n- [ ] #13 telemetria e privacidade\n- [ ] #15 integração site_simpleti\n\n## Dependências críticas\n1. #9 precede qualquer distribuição.\n2. #11 precede #8.\n3. #15 e a issue espelho no site precedem #3.\n4. #3 precede a troca final do cliente para #4.\n5. #4 precede remover OpenRouter direto do beta.\n6. #5 e #12 devem fechar antes da versão 1.0.\n7. #10 só inicia após contratos estáveis de #3 e #4.\n\n## Definition of Done global\n- Implementação revisada e documentada.\n- Unitários, integração, sistema, regressão e segurança verdes.\n- Benchmark sem regressão fora do orçamento.\n- Cobertura mínima de 85% no código novo.\n- Nenhum segredo ou dado de projeto em cliente, logs ou telemetria.\n- Evidência de CI anexada ao PR.\n- Rollback e migração testados.\n- Release assinada, com SBOM e notas.\n\n## Fora de escopo imediato\n- Expor assinaturas ou chaves de provedores ao usuário.\n- Permitir BYOK no produto final.\n- Revelar o slug interno do modelo na interface.

## Atualização arquitetural — integração Loop e gate real de produto

Novas issues release-blocking:

- [ ] #42 — integrar o `simplicio-loop` por contrato e materializar agentes dedicados;
- [ ] #43 — certificar a jornada completa instalada até delivery confirmado.

A existência de implementações parciais, mocks, shims, documentos ou PRs por componente não satisfaz essas duas issues. `#43` é o gate terminal da versão 1.0.

## Ordem real de dependências para 1.0

```text
Segurança e qualidade
#9 -> #11 -> corrigir #28/#29 e preflight #32

Operações e contexto
#5 + #6 + #12
       |
       v
Orquestração produtiva
#42 -> (#30/#31 evoluem sobre a base comprovada)

Identidade e serviço
#15 -> #3 -> #4

Distribuição e privacidade
#7 + #8 + #10 + #13 + #14

Todas as trilhas
       |
       v
#43 Product E2E
       |
       v
release 1.0
```

## Novos gates globais

- [ ] #42 comprova agentes concretos e independentes, não apenas roles documentados.
- [ ] #42 comprova fronteiras Code/Runtime/Loop/Mapper/Agent sem responsabilidade duplicada.
- [ ] #43 executa o mesmo fluxo em TUI, headless, ACP e workspace.
- [ ] #43 começa por instalação limpa e termina em delivery remotamente confirmado.
- [ ] #43 usa Runtime, Mapper e Loop reais/pinados nos cenários obrigatórios.
- [ ] Falha, crash, restart, rollback e regressão não geram falso COMPLETE.
- [ ] Nenhuma tag 1.0 pode ser criada com #42 ou #43 aberta.
- [ ] Fechamento exige unitários, contract, integração, sistema/E2E, regressão, segurança, caos, performance, cobertura e receipts reproduzíveis.

title: feat: Simplicio Code 0.3 beta com Runtime e identidade Brasil
body:
## O que mudou
- substitui a marca pública Grok Build por Simplicio Code nas superfícies rastreadas
- adiciona o tema padrão Simplicio Brasil em verde e amarelo
- publica o alias Simplicio-1 para tencent/hy3:free via OpenRouter
- lê a credencial somente de OPENROUTER_API_KEY, sem segredo no código
- inicia simplicio runtime map em segundo plano ao abrir uma pasta
- mantém toda leitura de arquivo fail-closed via Simplicio Runtime MCP
- define a versão 0.3.0-beta.1 e documenta a transição futura para login/gateway

## Impacto
A pasta selecionada passa a ser mapeada automaticamente pelo Runtime e todas as leituras existentes continuam atravessando o contrato MCP. O nome do provedor fica oculto na interface pelo alias Simplicio-1.

## Validação
- cargo check nos crates Runtime, tools, models, render e shell
- cargo test -p simplicio-runtime-client --lib: 2 passed, 1 ignored
- cargo test -p xai-grok-models --lib
- cargo fmt --all -- --check
- git diff --check
- scanner do segredo publicado: nenhuma ocorrência no worktree
- string exata Grok Build: nenhuma ocorrência rastreada

## Limitações beta
A chave fornecida foi recusada pelo OpenRouter com 401 e deve ser rotacionada. O beta aceita somente OPENROUTER_API_KEY por ambiente para desenvolvimento; o gateway e login Simplicio estão planejados em #3, #4, #9 e #15.

title: docs+feat: define site_simpleti client contract (part of #15)
body:
Part of #15.

## Context

Issue #15 asks to integrate `site_simpleti` as the account/subscription
backend for Simplicio Code (device login, entitlement, device management,
webhooks). That backend lives in a separate repository (`site_simpleti`)
that this agent has no access to build, run, or modify — so this PR
deliberately does **not** attempt any of the actual backend integration.
Instead it delivers the one slice that's genuinely completable and
verifiable from inside `simplicio-code`: the **client-side contract**, kept
decoupled per the issue's own stated goal ("mantendo o repositório
simplicio-code desacoplado por contrato").

## What's included (verified)

- `docs/contracts/site-simpleti-openapi.yaml` — OpenAPI 3.1 spec for
  `POST /auth/device/authorize`, `POST /auth/device/token`,
  `POST /auth/token/refresh`, `POST /auth/token/revoke`, `GET /entitlement`,
  `GET /devices`, `DELETE /devices/{deviceId}`, and
  `POST /webhooks/subscription`, matching the acceptance criteria: short-lived
  single-use device codes (`expires_in` capped, single-use token exchange),
  no leaking of upstream provider/model names in entitlement/profile fields,
  and idempotent webhook/revocation semantics.
- `docs/contracts/site-simpleti-api.md` — rationale for the contract,
  explicit invariants, and an honest list of what's still open.
- `crates/codegen/simplicio-account-client` — new workspace crate:
  - `schema.rs`: typed `serde` structs mirroring the OpenAPI schemas
    field-for-field (device flow, tokens, entitlement, devices, webhook
    event), so a future HTTP client has ready-made types instead of loose
    JSON. Includes round-trip tests against literal example payloads, plus
    a guard test that asserts entitlement profile names never contain an
    upstream vendor term (grok/openrouter/opencode/xai).
  - `idempotency.rs`: a pure, I/O-free implementation of the "process a
    webhook `event_id` at most once" rule (`SeenEventStore` trait +
    in-memory impl + `decide()`), with unit tests for duplicate delivery,
    out-of-order redelivery, distinct events, and that `mark_seen` is never
    re-invoked for a duplicate.
  - Registered in the root `Cargo.toml` workspace members / dependency alias
    section, following the existing `simplicio-runtime-client` crate's
    conventions.

### Test evidence

```
cargo test -p simplicio-account-client
...
running 10 tests
test schema::tests::device_token_error_uses_rfc8628_error_codes ... ok
test schema::tests::entitlement_response_never_requires_upstream_provider_field ... ok
test schema::tests::webhook_event_round_trips ... ok
test idempotency::tests::distinct_event_ids_are_each_processed_once ... ok
test idempotency::tests::duplicate_decisions_never_call_mark_seen_again ... ok
test idempotency::tests::empty_store_reports_empty ... ok
test idempotency::tests::first_delivery_of_an_event_id_is_processed ... ok
test idempotency::tests::redelivery_of_the_same_event_id_is_skipped ... ok
test schema::tests::device_authorize_response_matches_openapi_example ... ok
test idempotency::tests::out_of_order_redelivery_is_still_detected_as_duplicate ... ok

test result: ok. 10 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
```

`cargo clippy -p simplicio-account-client --all-targets` and
`cargo metadata --no-deps` both pass clean with the new crate registered.

## What remains open (most of the issue — cross-repo)

- The actual `site_simpleti` backend implementation (all endpoints above).
- A real HTTP client in this repo calling that backend (today this repo only
  has an MCP client for the local Runtime — a different protocol entirely —
  see `crates/codegen/simplicio-runtime-client`).
- `simplicio-code login` actually driving a browser through the device flow.
- Refresh-token persistence in the OS keychain.
- Entitlement/quota enforcement in the (not-yet-existing) inference gateway
  path (`POST /v1/code/responses`).
- End-to-end contract tests against a real or mock `site_simpleti` instance.
- **Issue #15 step 9** ("abrir issues espelho no site_simpleti com links
  cruzados"): **not done**. This agent has no access to the `site_simpleti`
  repository and cannot open an issue there. Please open a mirror issue in
  `site_simpleti` manually, linking back to #15 and to this PR, so the two
  repos can converge on the contract in `docs/contracts/site-simpleti-api.md`.

See `docs/contracts/site-simpleti-api.md` for the full "what's open" list.

title: feat(#8): signed beta release pipeline, checksum-verified installers, manifest signature verification
body:
Part of #8 — Publicar 0.3.0-beta.1 de forma reproduzível e criar atualização segura para macOS, Linux e Windows.

## What this PR actually does (a scoped, verified slice — not full completion)

Before writing anything, I checked what already existed: this repo has **no** `.github/workflows/` at all, but it *does* inherit a large (~5,800-line) auto-updater (`crates/codegen/xai-grok-update`) and installer (`crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager/scripts/install.sh`) from the upstream x.ai `grok-build` product — both still point at x.ai's own GCS bucket / GitHub repo / npm package, with **zero checksum or signature verification** on downloaded binaries (only a smoke-test run). Rewiring that entire subsystem safely, with its own test suite, is out of scope for one session — I left it untouched and documented it below instead of touching something I couldn't fully re-verify.

What I added:

- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — on `v*` tag push (or manual dispatch), builds `simplicio-code` for **Linux (x86_64)** and **macOS (aarch64)** (both real `dtolnay/rust-toolchain` + DotSlash + `cargo build --release` jobs), generates `SHA256SUMS.txt`, a dependency SBOM, and an Ed25519-signed release manifest, then publishes everything as a GitHub **prerelease**. A Windows build job is included but marked `continue-on-error: true` — see limitations below for why it currently can't succeed.
- **`scripts/release/generate_sbom.py`** — stdlib-only Python, walks `cargo metadata`'s resolved dependency graph from `xai-grok-pager-bin` and emits a CycloneDX-shaped SBOM (name/version/purl/license per crate). No `cargo-cyclonedx`/`syft` available in this environment, so this is a deliberately minimal substitute.
- **`scripts/release/generate_manifest.py`** — builds the signed `manifest.json` (version, channel, per-platform filename+sha256) from a directory of built artifacts.
- **`scripts/release/{sign_manifest.sh,gen_dev_key.sh}`** — Ed25519 signing via `openssl pkeyutl`. **These generate a throwaway keypair per CI run — clearly documented as a placeholder, not production key custody** (see file headers and release notes).
- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-update/src/manifest_verify.rs`** — the verification side: `verify_manifest_signature` (Ed25519, via `ring`) and `verify_artifact_checksum` (SHA-256). New, additive module — not yet wired into the live `auto_update.rs` flow (see limitations). 9 unit tests: valid manifest, tampered body, corrupted signature, wrong-key signature, checksum match/mismatch, truncated artifact, malformed base64 input, artifact lookup by platform.
- **`install.sh` / `install.ps1`** at the repo root — install *this* repo's own GitHub releases (not x.ai's). Verifies `SHA256SUMS.txt` first, and — when a signed manifest is present and verifies — cross-checks the manifest's checksum too, so an attacker who substitutes both the artifact *and* `SHA256SUMS.txt` (but doesn't have the signing key) is still caught.
- **`RELEASE_NOTES_0.3.0-beta.1.md`** — Runtime requirement, login/auth status, and an explicit "known limitations" section (copied into "Still open" below).

## What was actually verified (not just written)

This sandbox **cannot build the full Rust workspace at all**, on any branch — `cargo test`/`cargo check` on any crate depending on `xai-grok-tools-api` fails with `/dev/stdout: No such file or directory`, because `crates/build/xai-proto-build/src/lib.rs` (around line 119-120) hardcodes `--dependency_out=/dev/stdout --descriptor_set_out=/dev/null` as protoc arguments, and native Windows `protoc.exe` can't open those paths. I confirmed this by `git stash`-ing all my changes and re-running `cargo check -p xai-grok-tools-api` on a clean checkout — same failure, so it's pre-existing and unrelated to this PR (and it's also why `bin/protoc`'s DotSlash manifest has no `windows-x86_64` entry — Windows was never wired up for this build step). I'm flagging this as a real, separate bug rather than fixing it here.

Given that, I validated the new logic for real, just outside the broken workspace:
- Copied `manifest_verify.rs` into a standalone scratch crate with the exact same `ring`/`sha2`/`base64`/`serde_json` dependency versions this workspace uses — all 9 unit tests pass.
- Cross-checked that a manifest **signed by `openssl pkeyutl` (what CI will use) verifies correctly against the `ring`-based Rust verifier** — proved with a real signed manifest read from disk by a small example binary, plus a tamper test (flipping a byte correctly gets rejected).
- Ran `scripts/release/generate_sbom.py` against this repo's real `cargo metadata` — produced a real 1265-component SBOM (found and fixed a Windows-console UTF-8 decoding bug in the process).
- Ran `install.sh` end-to-end against a local fake HTTP release (not github.com) with a real signed manifest: (1) happy path installs correctly with both checksum and manifest-signature checks passing; (2) a truncated/corrupted artifact is rejected before install, nothing written; (3) a substituted artifact + rewritten `SHA256SUMS.txt` (simulating a mirror/MITM compromise without the signing key) is correctly rejected because the signed manifest's checksum disagrees — found and fixed a real bug in this same test pass: `sha256sum`'s ` *filename` (asterisk = binary mode) output wasn't matching my initial grep pattern, so checksum lookup silently failed; switched to an `awk` field match that strips the leading `*`.
- Validated `install.ps1` and `release.yml` with PowerShell's own parser / PyYAML respectively (syntax only — no Windows/Actions runner available here).

## Still open (explicitly out of scope for this change)

- **Real production code signing.** The Ed25519 key is generated fresh per CI run and discarded — proves the mechanism, not a trust root. Real key custody (HSM or a durable, access-controlled CI secret, rotation, revocation, and how the trusted public key ships inside the binary) is an org/infra decision.
- **Windows build.** Currently fails due to the `/dev/stdout`/`/dev/null` protoc-argument bug in `xai-proto-build` (pre-existing, not touched here) and the missing `windows-x86_64` entry in `bin/protoc`'s DotSlash manifest.
- **The in-app auto-updater still points at x.ai infra.** `xai-grok-update`'s `auto_update.rs` (GCS bucket `grok-build-public-artifacts`, GitHub repo `xai-org-shared/grok-build`) and `crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager/scripts/install.sh` (same) were not repointed at simplicio-code's own releases — that's a large, separately-tested subsystem; `manifest_verify.rs` is built additively so it can be wired in later without churn, but I didn't do that wiring here.
- **No gradual/percentage rollout or automated rollback state machine.** Only a plain GitHub prerelease publish.
- **No real multi-OS CI run yet** — this workflow hasn't executed on GitHub Actions (would happen on the first tag push); I validated the underlying steps locally instead, as described above.
- **SBOM is minimal** (dependency list + license, not a full SPDX/CycloneDX toolchain).

## Test plan

- [x] `manifest_verify.rs` unit tests pass in a standalone crate with the real dependency versions (ring/sha2/base64/serde_json)
- [x] OpenSSL-signed manifest verifies via the `ring`-based Rust verifier (cross-tool compatibility)
- [x] `generate_sbom.py` produces a real SBOM from this repo's `cargo metadata`
- [x] `install.sh` happy path, tampered-artifact rejection, and substituted-artifact-plus-checksums-file rejection, all against a local fake release server
- [x] `install.ps1` / `release.yml` syntax-checked
- [ ] Full `cargo test -p xai-grok-update` in-repo — blocked by a pre-existing, unrelated protoc build bug in this sandbox (see above); would need a Linux/macOS CI runner or a fixed `xai-proto-build` to actually execute
- [ ] Actual GitHub Actions run of `release.yml` — needs a real tag push to observe

title: perf: token/latency benchmark harness for Runtime reads
body:
Part of #12.

## What this adds

- `crates/codegen/simplicio-perf-bench`: a versioned golden-task corpus (5 fixtures: small file, medium file, 4-deep monorepo-nested file, deterministic 4MiB synthetic large file, invalid path) with a `simplicio-bench` binary that measures wall-clock latency (p50/p95/mean/stdev) and an approximate `bytes/4` token count for two real code paths:
  - `direct_read` — `std::fs::read`, the same primitive `LocalFs` uses.
  - `runtime_attempt` — the real `simplicio-runtime-client` MCP client, exactly as `SimplicioRuntimeFs::read_file` calls it.
- `simplicio-bench-check` binary + `find_regressions()` (7 passing unit tests): flags any task/path where p50 latency or approx tokens regress >10%, or where success rate drops at all (checked first, per the issue's correctness-before-tokens acceptance criteria).
- `scripts/bench/run.sh` reproducible runner.
- `docs/perf/token-latency-benchmark.md` — methodology + the actual measured results below.

## What was actually measured (real numbers, not projected)

Ran `./target/debug/simplicio-bench.exe --repeats 1` on the dev machine. Full JSON committed at `crates/codegen/simplicio-perf-bench/baselines/run_2026-07-17.json`.

| Task | Path | Success | Latency (ms) | approx tokens |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `read_small` (~460B) | direct_read | 1/1 | 0.60 | 94.25 |
| `read_small` | runtime_attempt | 1/1 | 6439.19 | n/a |
| `read_medium` (~10.6KB) | direct_read | 1/1 | 0.22 | 2217.5 |
| `read_medium` | runtime_attempt | 1/1 | 6198.29 | n/a |
| `read_monorepo_nested` | direct_read | 1/1 | 0.43 | 84.5 |
| `read_monorepo_nested` | runtime_attempt | 1/1 | 5020.66 | n/a |
| `read_large_synthetic` (4MiB) | direct_read | 1/1 | 2.01 | 1,048,576 |
| `read_large_synthetic` | runtime_attempt | 0/1 | 10457.63 | n/a |
| `read_invalid_path` | direct_read | 0/1 (expected) | 0.06 | n/a |
| `read_invalid_path` | runtime_attempt | 0/1 (expected) | 4456.98 | n/a |

**Important caveats, stated explicitly rather than glossed over:**

- **N=1 per cell**, not the 20+ repeats the harness defaults to. Each `runtime_attempt` call spawns a fresh process and does a full MCP handshake against a real installed Runtime binary, which took 4–10 seconds *per call* — the default 20-repeat run (200 process spawns across 5 tasks × 2 paths) did not finish in the time available for this change. Treat these as single anecdotal data points, not trends. `scripts/bench/run.sh --repeats 20` is the documented next step.
- The `runtime_attempt` numbers came from a Simplicio Runtime binary (v3.5.2) that happened to be installed on the dev machine's `PATH` — **it is not built by this repo** (`crates/codegen/simplicio-runtime-client` only ships the MCP client, `resolve_binary()` literally errors `RuntimeNotFound` with nothing on PATH/`SIMPLICIO_BIN`). So this is not a CI-reproducible baseline; a fresh checkout of this repo with no Runtime installed will see every `runtime_attempt` fail closed instead.
- The harness pays full process-spawn + handshake cost on every call (no client reuse), so the ~5–10s runtime_attempt latencies are a cold-path worst case, not a fair "steady-state" comparison against `SimplicioRuntimeFs`'s warm client reuse.
- `approx_tokens` is only computed for `direct_read` today — the Runtime response content isn't threaded back out of the bench binary yet, so no token-savings comparison is possible yet, only latency/success comparison.

## What remains open (blocked or out of scope for this change)

- The full CI-reproducible Runtime/Mapper-vs-direct-read comparison (cold/warm/incremental, Mapper token savings, "concise mode") — blocked on a Runtime/Mapper implementation this repo builds or pins (tracked as #5/#6). See `docs/perf/token-latency-benchmark.md` "Scope and what's blocked".
- A statistically meaningful sample size (re-run with `--repeats 20+` on a machine with time to spare).
- Root-causing why `read_large_synthetic` failed against the real Runtime.
- Threading Runtime read content back out so token counts are comparable both ways.
- Wiring `simplicio-bench-check` into CI against a pinned baseline/runner.
- Macro-level (whole-session) benchmarking beyond the single file-read primitive.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p simplicio-perf-bench` — 7 unit tests pass (regression-gate logic, deterministic fixture generation, golden-task id uniqueness).
- [x] `cargo build -p simplicio-perf-bench` — builds cleanly.
- [x] Ran `simplicio-bench` for real and captured output (see table above and committed baseline JSON).
- [ ] Not done: a 20+ repeat run (see caveats above) and CI wiring.

title: Mapper contract/cache slice (#6) + brand and theme-contrast audit (#7)
body:
Part of #6. Part of #7.

Both are large, multi-week epics. This PR is one focused, verified slice of each — not a full close.

## Part of #6 — Mapper as incremental context

**Added:** `crates/codegen/simplicio-runtime-client/src/map_cache.rs` — the versioned contract, cache, and budget primitives the issue asks for, as a genuinely functional vertical slice (not a scaffold):

- Versioned contract: `MapResult` tagged `schema: "simplicio.map-result/v1"` (`MAP_RESULT_SCHEMA_V1`). Anything with the wrong schema, or a value whose `repo_hash`/`runtime_version` don't match its own cache key, is treated as a cache miss — never served.
- Five-state lifecycle exactly per the issue: `MapState::{Waiting, Mapping, Ready, Degraded, Failed}`. `MapResult::is_usable()` is `true` only for `Ready`/`Degraded`, so "failure never falls back to reading outside the Runtime" is enforced at the type level.
- Disk+memory cache (`MapCache`) keyed by `(repo_hash, runtime_version)`, with write-dedup (identical value ⇒ no redundant disk write, verified via unchanged file mtime) and `invalidate_repo()` that drops every Runtime-version entry for a repo, in memory and on disk.
- `compute_repo_hash()`: a `blake3` digest of git `HEAD` ref + branch name + canonicalized worktree root, resolving the `.git` `gitdir:` indirection so worktrees get their own hash. Branch switches, worktree switches, and Runtime upgrades all naturally miss the old cache entry. The hash never contains the raw path (tested).
- `budgeted_summary(summary, budget_chars)`: truncates to a fixed character budget without ever splitting a multi-byte char, with a visible truncation marker.

**Evidence:**
```
$ cargo test -p simplicio-runtime-client
running 23 tests
test result: ok. 22 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
```
(the 1 ignored test predates this change and needs a real installed Runtime binary). `cargo clippy -p simplicio-runtime-client --all-targets` is clean except 2 pre-existing warnings in `lib.rs` unrelated to this change.

**Explicitly open** (see `docs/mapper-context.md` for the full breakdown): wiring the budgeted summary into `xai-grok-agent`'s actual prompt context; watcher-driven incremental updates; TUI/headless cancel/reindex/diagnostics UI; concurrent-session locking; cold/warm/incremental benchmarks; the 85% coordinator-coverage target (no "coordinator" component exists yet — this is the primitives it would sit on); integration/system tests against real repos.

## Part of #7 — brand and theme audit

**Fixed** the three highest-visibility "Grok"/xAI leaks:
- `crates/codegen/xai-grok-agent/src/prompt/context.rs:153` — `DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT_LABEL` was `"Grok"`, now `"Simplicio Code"` (this is what the model says when asked "what are you").
- `crates/codegen/xai-grok-agent/templates/prompt.md:1` — dropped `"released by xAI"` from the literal system prompt (re-encrypted via `python3 scripts/encrypt_templates.py`, matching `prompt_encrypted.rs`'s existing anti-staleness test).
- `crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager/src/app/dispatch/status.rs:361` — the desktop "session ready" notification title was `"Grok"`, now `"Simplicio Code"`.

**Found and fixed a real WCAG AA violation** (not just eyeballed) in the default "Simplicio Brasil" theme: `crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager-render/src/theme/groknight.rs` used the literal Brazil-flag navy `#002776` as the foreground for `accent_system`/`accent_skill`/`fuzzy_accent`/`accent_verify` — computed contrast against both theme backgrounds was **~1.4:1**, far under the 4.5:1 AA minimum for text. Swapped to the already-defined `BLUE1` (`#3A95AB`, **5.3–5.7:1**, comfortably AA). Every other semantic color already passed (worst case 4.81:1). Verified:
```
$ python3 scripts/contrast_audit.py
... 7/7 [OK], worst case accent_success 4.81:1 on bg_base (need >= 4.5:1)
[contrast-audit] PASS
```

**Added automated tooling** (both currently pass, and both actually run — not just written):
- `scripts/brand_audit.py`: strict zero-tolerance scan of README/CONTRIBUTING/SECURITY/docs/prompt-templates for old-brand tokens (small, rationale-carrying allowlist for real internal-path references), plus a regression guard over `crates/**/*.rs` string literals (currently 2035 "Grok"/"xAI" occurrences — the full rebrand is out of scope this session, but this locks the number from growing).
- `scripts/contrast_audit.py`: computed WCAG contrast ratios for the default theme's semantic colors.

**Explicitly open** (full breakdown with rationale in `docs/brand-audit.md`): `~/.grok/` config dir, `auth.x.ai` OAuth host, `grok.com` relay, and most of `xai-grok-pager/src/app/cli.rs` help text — these are functional integration points, not just strings, and renaming them needs a coordinated migration so existing installs don't break; ~40 `xai-grok-*` crate/package names (pure mechanical rename, large diff, no behavior change); "SpaceXAI" in `LICENSE`/`CONTRIBUTING.md` (a deliberate, consistent, repo-wide choice — flagged as a business/legal decision, not silently changed); light theme (`grokday.rs`) still uses the original blue/magenta palette, not Brazil green/yellow (a design decision, not a rename); THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES/LICENSE line-by-line review not performed.

## Known blocker (documented, not fabricated)

`crates/codegen/xai-grok-tools-api/build.rs` requires `protoc` via a `dotslash` pointer file (`bin/protoc`); this sandbox has neither `protoc` nor `dotslash` installed, and no matching binary exists anywhere on disk (verified: `bin/protoc` is a dotslash manifest, not an executable). Every crate that transitively depends on `xai-grok-tools-api` — including `xai-grok-agent`, `xai-grok-pager`, and `xai-grok-pager-render`, i.e. every crate touched for the three brand-string fixes and the theme-contrast fix — **could not be compiled or test-run in this environment**. This is pre-existing and unrelated to this PR's changes. Those edits were kept intentionally minimal (single string-literal swaps, and a const-to-const same-type color reference matching an existing pattern already used elsewhere in the same file) and reviewed line-by-line against every test in the repo that references the touched constants (`grep -rn "system_prompt_label\|DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT_LABEL\|released by xAI"` across the workspace — no test asserts the literal old string, only the constant), but **a reviewer with a working `protoc` must run `cargo test -p xai-grok-agent -p xai-grok-pager -p xai-grok-pager-render` before merging.** The one crate genuinely unaffected by this blocker, `simplicio-runtime-client`, was built and tested successfully (see above).

Full detail on both fronts, including exact file:line references for every "fixed" vs "open" item, is in `docs/mapper-context.md` and `docs/brand-audit.md`.

title: [bug] cargo test warns that xai-grok-pager-bin main.rs is present in multiple build targets
body:
## Context

A targeted telemetry test passed, but Cargo emitted a workspace configuration warning that indicates the pager binary's entrypoint is registered in more than one target.

## Reproduction

From the repository root:

```sh
cargo test -p xai-computer-hub-sdk log_donate --locked --no-fail-fast
```

Observed on 2026-07-17:
```
warning: crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager-bin/Cargo.toml: file
crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager-bin/src/main.rs found to be present in multiple build targets
```

The command still completed with:
```
test result: ok. 7 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 190 filtered out
```

## Expected

The package manifest declares each source file exactly once for the intended binary/example target, with no ambiguous or duplicate target discovery.

## Impact

- The warning can hide a real target-layout mistake.
- Cargo may compile, test, package, or publish the same entrypoint under multiple target identities.
- CI output becomes noisy and makes fail-on-warning policies unreliable.
- Release artifacts and binary smoke tests may exercise a different target than expected.
- The warning was encountered while validating issue #13 telemetry, so it can affect unrelated lanes and the quality matrix in issue #11.

## Investigation steps

1. Inspect `crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager-bin/Cargo.toml` for explicit `[[bin]]`, `[[example]]`, `autobins`, or target paths pointing to the same `src/main.rs`.
2. Run `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` and enumerate the package target list.
3. Run `cargo test -p xai-grok-pager-bin --locked --all-targets` and `cargo build -p xai-grok-pager-bin --locked --release` to verify the intended target set.
4. Check workspace-level manifests and generated target metadata for a second registration.
5. Confirm the fix on macOS, Linux, and Windows CI.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `cargo metadata --format-version 1` lists the pager `main.rs` exactly once for the intended executable target.
2. `cargo test --workspace --locked --all-targets` emits no duplicate-target warning for `xai-grok-pager-bin`.
3. The intended binary still builds and runs `--version` or the documented smoke command.
4. Unit/integration tests cover the package target layout and do not rely on an accidentally duplicated target.
5. Release packaging includes exactly one pager executable with the expected name and no duplicate artifact.
6. CI quality gates treat a recurrence as a failure or otherwise surface it as an actionable diagnostic.
7. macOS, Linux, and Windows builds are verified.
8. The issue includes before/after `cargo metadata` and build/test evidence.
9. New or changed manifest logic has at least 85% applicable test/verification coverage, with no unrelated dependency changes.

## Non-goals

- Do not rename the public binary without a migration plan.
- Do not suppress the warning blindly.
- Do not delete a target until the release and smoke-test behavior is proven.

title: security(ci): secret-scan/lint/deny CI gate + fix raw api_key log leak
body:
Part of #9. Part of #11.

## Summary

A scoped, verified slice of both issues: a real secret-leak fix, plus the CI quality/security gate that didn't exist at all in this repo (`.github/` was empty before this PR).

## What's done (with evidence)

### Issue #9 (secrets/redaction)

- **Fixed a real leak**: `crates/codegen/xai-grok-sampler/src/client.rs` logged the raw `api_key` via `tracing::debug!` on the invalid-header-value error path (both `XApiKey` and `Bearer` auth schemes), bypassing the redaction layer entirely. Now logs only `api_key_len`/`api_key_is_ascii`. Added `invalid_api_key_error_path_never_logs_raw_secret`, a regression test using a hand-written capturing `tracing::Subscriber` that asserts the raw secret never appears in any emitted field. Verified the `Subscriber`/`Visit` logic standalone (this workspace requires `protoc` on PATH, which isn't available by default in every dev environment) — passes.
- **Confirmed existing redaction coverage is real, not just tests**: audited call sites of `xai_grok_secrets::redact_secrets`/`redact_json_string_values`/`redact_user_paths` — genuinely wired into the OTel export path (`otel_layer/mod.rs`), the external/customer telemetry pipeline, and the Sentry `before_send` scrubber. `cargo test -p xai-grok-secrets` passes (16/16).
- **Secret scan of source + reachable git history**: no live-looking secrets found. Every match for realistic secret shapes (`sk-`, `xai-`, `AKIA`, `ghp_`, `AIza`, PEM blocks, JWTs, generic `key=`/`token=` assignments) resolves to a test fixture, doc placeholder, or `.env.example` template — see the audit notes in `docs/security/incident-runbook.md` §1. Ran `gitleaks detect` (installed locally, v8.30.1) against full history: 87 findings, all in files that no longer exist in the tree (inherited from the pre-Rust-rewrite ancestor project) and all entropy false-positives on hashes/checksums — none are real secrets, none are in the current source tree.
- **`docs/security/incident-runbook.md`**: detection → immediate response → scoping → communication → recovery, plus the required-tests list from the issue's acceptance criteria and an explicit "known gaps" section.
- **`.githooks/pre-commit`** (opt-in, `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`): local gitleaks + `cargo fmt --check` pre-check. No hook framework existed in this repo (no husky/pre-commit-py) — added a plain-shell one rather than a new dependency.

### Issue #11 (CI matrix)

- **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** (new — no CI existed in this repo before this PR): `fmt`, `clippy -D warnings`, `check`, `test` on ubuntu/macos; a diff-scoped `gitleaks` secret-scan job; a `cargo-deny` job (advisories/licenses/bans/sources).
- **`deny.toml`**: not written blind — validated against a real `cargo deny check all` run against this workspace's actual `Cargo.lock`. `cargo deny check all` exits 0 locally. Pre-existing `unmaintained`/`unsound`/`vulnerability` RUSTSEC advisories (14 unmaintained/unsound + 4 vulnerability IDs, none introduced by this PR) are individually baselined in the ignore list with crate names in comments — not fixed (would mean bumping deps across 60+ crates, unverifiable in this environment) and not silently hidden.
- **`.gitleaks.toml`**: extends gitleaks' default ruleset; allowlists only the specific redaction-test-fixture files that intentionally hold secret-shaped canary strings.
- Verified locally: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` (clean), `cargo clippy -p xai-grok-secrets --all-targets -- -D warnings` (clean), `cargo test -p xai-grok-secrets` (16/16 pass), `cargo deny check all` (exit 0), `gitleaks detect` against the new commit with `--log-opts` scoped to just this commit (0 findings).

## What's still open / explicitly out of scope

- **Actual key revocation/rotation**: not attempted — this is an operational action outside what a code change can perform or prove. No live-looking secrets were found to rotate in the first place (see scan above).
- **Windows build gap (discovered, not fixed)**: `crates/build/xai-proto-build/src/lib.rs:119` hardcodes `--dependency_out=/dev/stdout` / `--descriptor_set_out=/dev/null` when invoking `protoc`, which are POSIX-only paths — any crate transitively depending on `xai-grok-tools-api` (including `xai-grok-sampler`, `xai-grok-telemetry`) fails to build natively on Windows. The CI Windows job is honestly scoped to `xai-grok-secrets` only (provably protoc-free) instead of claiming full coverage. Flagged as a follow-up task.
- **`SamplerConfig` derives `Debug`** with a plain `api_key: Option<String>` field — a latent risk (would leak the raw key if anything ever does `{:?}` on it) though no current call site does. Not fixed here to keep this PR scoped; flagged as a follow-up task.
- **Centralized secret-manager/gateway**: doesn't exist in this repo; secrets are supplied via client-side env/config today. Out of scope for a single PR per the issue's own framing.
- **Coverage threshold (85%)**: no coverage tool is wired into this workspace; adding one plus reaching an 85% threshold across new modules is a larger effort than this PR's scope — noted as a gap rather than fabricated.
- **PTY/TUI/ACP e2e surfaces, MCP runtime tests, macOS/Windows/Linux full matrix parity, cache quota/expiration**: not attempted; would need real infra (runners, MCP servers) this environment doesn't have.
- **Branch protection requiring these checks**: requires repo-admin access; not something this PR can configure.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p xai-grok-secrets` — 16/16 pass
- [x] `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — clean
- [x] `cargo clippy -p xai-grok-secrets --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo deny check all` — exit 0 (advisories/bans/licenses/sources all `ok`)
- [x] `gitleaks detect -c .gitleaks.toml --log-opts="HEAD~1..HEAD"` on the new commit — 0 findings
- [x] Standalone probe of the new `tracing::Subscriber`-based regression test's logic (outside the full workspace, which needs `protoc`) — passes
- [ ] `cargo test -p xai-grok-sampler` (the crate actually touched) — blocked locally by the Windows protoc bug above; will run in CI on ubuntu/macos where it's expected to pass
- [ ] Full `.github/workflows/ci.yml` run — not yet observed on GitHub Actions infra (first PR to add CI to this repo)

title: feat: additive auth state machine + private gateway client (issues #3, #4)
body:
Part of #3
Part of #4

## What this actually is

A single new, additive, standalone crate — `crates/codegen/simplicio-code-gateway` — that is **not wired into any production request path**. It compiles, has a full test suite that passes, and is a genuine client-side implementation of both contracts, but it does not replace the current OpenRouter-based default model routing (`crates/codegen/xai-grok-models/default_models.json`) or the existing `AuthManager`/`TokenRefresher` machinery in `xai-grok-shell`. That swap-over is real, cross-cutting surgery across a very large existing config/auth surface (`xai-grok-shell/src/agent/config.rs` alone is >10k lines with hundreds of existing tests asserting current `base_url`/BYOK behavior) and is out of scope for one session without a live backend to validate against. I found and reverted an in-progress edit to `default_models.json` that had started that swap without proving it safe — see "What I found and reverted" below.

## Issue #3 — auth/entitlement (`src/auth.rs`)

- `AuthState`: `SignedOut` / `Authorized` / `Revoked`.
- Device-code login (`IdentityClient::begin_device_authorization` / `poll_device_authorization`) — no browser required, so it works identically for TUI/headless/ACP callers (none of them get special-cased state).
- Refresh rotation: `AuthSession::rotate_refresh` replaces the stored refresh token; the fake backend (`FakeIdentity`) enforces single-use polling to model this.
- Clock-skew-aware, fail-closed expiry: a 30s `CLOCK_SKEW` constant; both entitlement and access-token expiry require a margin past "now", not just `now < expires_at`.
- `logout()` revokes remotely (POST to `/v1/code/auth/session/revoke`) and clears the local secret regardless of whether the remote call succeeds.
- Entitlement (`plan`, `expires_at`, `max_request_tokens`, `max_tool_calls`) has no field that could name an upstream provider — asserted directly in `tests/contract.rs`.
- Storage: `MemorySecretStore` (tests) and `OsSecretStore` (real OS credential store via the `keyring` crate). **I verified `OsSecretStore` end-to-end against this machine's real Windows Credential Manager** — not just that it compiles:
  ```
  cargo test -p simplicio-code-gateway --lib -- --ignored os_secret_store
  running 1 test
  test auth::tests::os_secret_store_round_trips_a_real_secret_through_the_platform_keychain ... ok
  ```
  It's `#[ignore]`d by default (headless CI containers commonly have no credential-store backend), but it is a verified implementation, not an unverified stub.

### Acceptance criteria — status

- DONE: Nenhuma chave upstream no cliente/config/log/telemetria — no field for one exists in this crate; `redact_diagnostics` scrubs prompt/content/code/response/token/secret/api_key-shaped fields before any error message is built.
- DONE: Tokens expirados/revogados não autorizam novas requisições — fail-closed `access_token()` (tested for both expiry and revoke).
- DONE: Refresh rotacionado invalida o anterior — `rotate_refresh` unit test + fake backend enforces single-use.
- DONE: Logout remove segredo local e encerra sessão remota — `logout_revokes_remotely_and_clears_local_secret` integration test (real HTTP against wiremock).
- PARTIAL: TUI/headless/ACP com estados equivalentes — the state machine itself is UI-agnostic (same `AuthState` regardless of caller), but no TUI/headless/ACP surface actually calls into this crate yet — that's the "wire it in" work, not done here.
- DONE: API de entitlement retorna plano/limites/validade sem revelar provedor — asserted directly (`entitlement_response_fixture_matches_entitlement_schema_and_hides_provider`).
- PARTIAL: Login funciona nos três SOs sem browser — the device-code flow doesn't require a browser by construction, and `OsSecretStore` (via `keyring`) targets Windows Credential Manager / macOS Keychain / Secret Service, but I could only verify Windows in this sandbox. macOS/Linux are unverified by me this session.
- NOT DONE: Full CLI login UX, real backend integration — no backend exists; out of scope as instructed.

Test coverage: unit tests for the state machine (expiry, clock skew, rotation, logout) in `src/auth.rs`; a fake-backend integration test (`tests/fake_contract.rs`); wiremock-backed real-HTTP integration tests (`tests/wiremock_contract.rs`) for the device flow (pending -> granted, denied) and logout/revoke. No coverage tool (`cargo-tarpaulin`/`cargo-llvm-cov`) is installed in this sandbox, so I'm not claiming a coverage percentage — reporting pass/fail counts instead, honestly.

## Issue #4 — private gateway (`src/gateway.rs`, `openapi/simplicio-gateway.yaml`)

- `PrivateGateway::models()` / `.usage()` / `.chat_stream()` — GET/GET/POST against `/v1/code/models`, `/v1/code/usage`, `/v1/code/chat/completions`.
- Streaming: hand-rolled SSE parser (`parse_sse_events`) over `bytes_stream()`, forwarding `GatewayEvent { text_delta, tool_call, usage, done }` chunks; tool-call deltas are part of the wire shape (`ToolCall`), not bolted on separately.
- Cancellation: `tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken` raced against the byte stream inside the SSE loop — cancelling mid-stream surfaces `GatewayError::Cancelled` promptly, not "wait for drop and hope."
- `simplicio-1` is the only public model id (`PUBLIC_MODEL_ID` const), enforced in `ChatRequest::new` and rejected server-request-side otherwise.
- Errors: upstream failures collapse to `GatewayError::Server { status, message }` with `message` passed through `redact_diagnostics` first — verified in `unauthorized_401_is_a_stable_server_error_without_leaking_the_body` and `upstream_5xx_is_a_stable_server_error_without_naming_the_provider` that a raw body containing a token/provider-hostname never survives into the returned error.
- `base_url` construction rejects non-HTTPS URLs (except http+loopback, for tests) and userinfo-in-URL, so this crate cannot be pointed at an arbitrary/non-Simplicio host with embedded credentials by construction.

### Acceptance criteria — status

- DONE: Nenhuma credencial/hostname upstream no binário — no such field exists; `PrivateGateway` takes exactly one bearer (the Simplicio access token from `AuthSession`).
- DONE: Simplicio-1 é o único nome público inicial.
- DONE: Streaming/cancelamento/tool-calls end-to-end — `chat_stream_success_over_real_sse_http`, `chat_stream_is_cancellable_mid_stream` (real HTTP, real SSE, real cancellation).
- PARTIAL: Limites por usuário/assinatura — `GatewayLimits::enforce` exists and is unit-tested, but it's a client-side pre-check against a caller-supplied limit, not a server-enforced quota (no server exists yet).
- DONE: Falha upstream produz erro Simplicio estável — `GatewayError::Server{status,message}` + redaction, tested for 401/429/5xx.
- NOT DONE: Secret rotacionável sem nova versão do cliente — no secret-rotation mechanism exists server-side to test against; not applicable without a backend.
- DONE: Auditoria não guarda prompt/código/resposta por padrão — `redact_diagnostics` drops any field whose key contains prompt/content/code/response/argument/authorization/token/secret/api_key, tested in `redaction::tests::removes_payloads_and_secrets_but_keeps_metadata`.

Test coverage: OpenAPI contract (`openapi/simplicio-gateway.yaml`) covering all 8 paths this client actually calls (asserted in a test so the schema can't silently drift from the code); golden-payload tests (`tests/contract.rs`) deserializing fixtures into the real response types; wiremock integration tests (`tests/wiremock_contract.rs`) covering streaming, mid-stream cancellation, 401, 429 (with `Retry-After`), 5xx, and timeout. No SSRF/tenant-isolation tests were written — there's no multi-tenant server to isolate against yet; the base-URL scheme/userinfo validation is the closest analogue available client-side.

## Verified test run (this session, this machine)

```
cargo test -p simplicio-code-gateway
  running 7 tests   (src/auth.rs + src/gateway.rs + src/redaction.rs unit tests) ... 7 passed
  running 5 tests   (tests/contract.rs, golden payloads + schema-path check)     ... 5 passed
  running 1 test    (tests/fake_contract.rs)                                     ... 1 passed
  running 11 tests  (tests/wiremock_contract.rs, real HTTP against wiremock)     ... 11 passed
total: 24 passed, 0 failed

cargo test -p simplicio-code-gateway --lib -- --ignored os_secret_store
  1 passed (real Windows Credential Manager round-trip)

cargo clippy -p simplicio-code-gateway --all-targets
  clean, no warnings
```

No coverage tool is installed in this sandbox (`cargo-tarpaulin`/`cargo-llvm-cov` both absent) — I'm not claiming an 85% number without a tool that measured it.

## What I found and reverted

This worktree already had substantial uncommitted work from a prior pass at this exact task (this crate, largely as committed here) plus an in-progress edit to `crates/codegen/xai-grok-models/default_models.json` that pointed the real default-model config at an empty `base_url` and dropped `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` routing — i.e. it had started wiring the production default-model path toward "no BYOK" without a working gateway behind it and without running the very large existing `xai-grok-shell/src/agent/config.rs` test suite that asserts current `base_url`/credential-resolution behavior. I reverted that file to its committed state. Swapping the real default-model routing over to this gateway is real, valuable follow-up work, but it needs its own PR with `xai-grok-shell`'s existing config tests run and passing, plus a real backend to point at.

## Bugs fixed along the way (all in the new crate, none pre-existing on `main`)

- `tokio-util = { features = ["sync"] }` — that feature no longer exists in current `tokio-util` (0.7.17); `CancellationToken` etc. ship unconditionally now. Removed the stale feature request from both the workspace and this crate's `Cargo.toml`.
- `url::Url` doesn't implement `Serialize`/`Deserialize` without its `serde` feature — added `features = ["serde"]` to this crate's `url` dependency.
- `async_stream::try_stream!` + `?` inside a `tokio::select!` branch doesn't compose (the branches aren't directly in the macro's body) — rewrote `chat_stream`'s loop to resolve the `select!` to a plain `Result` first, then apply `?` outside it, in the macro body directly.
- `SecretString` had both a derived `Debug` and a manual `impl Debug` (E0119) — dropped the derive, kept the manual redacting impl.
- `SecretString::expose()` was private but called from another module in the same crate — made it `pub(crate)`.
- `expiry_skew_is_fail_closed` (pre-existing test) asserted `Err(AuthError::AccessExpired)` while varying *entitlement* expiry with a fixed 300s access-token TTL — it happened to compile and its assertion was simply wrong given the actual math (300s access-token TTL never expires in that scenario), so the test was silently checking nothing before I ran it. Fixed to actually construct a near-expiry access token so the skew-tolerance branch it claims to test is the one under test. Documented inline in the test.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build -p simplicio-code-gateway`
- [x] `cargo test -p simplicio-code-gateway` (24/24 passing)
- [x] `cargo test -p simplicio-code-gateway --lib -- --ignored os_secret_store` (real keychain round-trip, 1/1 passing)
- [x] `cargo clippy -p simplicio-code-gateway --all-targets` (clean)
- [x] `cargo metadata --no-deps` for the whole workspace (manifest resolves)
- [ ] Full-workspace `cargo check`/`cargo test` — blocked in this sandbox by an unrelated pre-existing issue (missing/incompatible `protoc` binary breaks `xai-grok-tools-api`'s build script); not something this PR's changes caused, and not something I could work around without touching unrelated infra.

title: [privacy] redact absolute source paths from crash reports before storage or upload
body:
## Context

The crash-handler test suite passes, but the current symbolication/reporting path appears to preserve absolute source filenames in human-readable crash reports.

Relevant code paths:
- `crates/codegen/xai-crash-handler/src/symbolicate.rs` converts `sym.filename()` with `display().to_string()`.
- `crates/codegen/xai-crash-handler/src/lib.rs::check_previous_crash` writes `report_text` directly to `last-crash-report.txt` and archives the same text under `history/`.

## Reproduction / evidence

From the repository root:

```sh
cargo test -p xai-crash-handler --locked --no-fail-fast
```

Observed on 2026-07-17:
- 11 unit tests passed;
- 6 integration tests passed with 1 ignored;
- 1 additional test passed;
- no crash-report privacy fixture failed because one is not present for absolute-path redaction.

Static inspection:
```rust
resolved.filename = sym.filename().map(|f| f.display().to_string());
```

and:
```rust
let report_text = symbolicate::format_report(&blob, &frames);
let _ = std::fs::write(&report_path, &report_text);
archive_report(crash_dir, &report_text, blob.timestamp);
```

## Expected

Crash reports may retain useful relative source locations and symbol names, but must never persist:
- the user's home directory;
- absolute workspace/project paths;
- usernames;
- environment values;
- command-line secrets;
- prompts, responses, or source content.

The same redaction policy must apply before local storage, archival, diagnostics listing, and any future upload.

## Impact

A local crash report is sensitive persisted telemetry. It may be copied into support bundles or uploaded later. Absolute paths reveal usernames, repository locations, and local project structure, and can violate the privacy contract in issue #13.

## Investigation steps

1. Build a crash blob fixture containing symbol frames with absolute paths under a synthetic home/workspace.
2. Run `check_previous_crash` against a temporary crash directory.
3. Inspect `last-crash-report.txt` and `history/crash-*.txt`.
4. Assert that no absolute path, home directory, username, environment value, or canary secret appears.
5. Verify that relative file/line information and symbol names remain useful.
6. Review Unix and Windows symbolication paths separately.

## Acceptance criteria

1. A single documented redaction function is applied to every crash-report rendering path before writing or uploading.
2. Absolute source paths are converted to stable relative/basename forms or a non-sensitive placeholder.
3. Home directories, usernames, workspace paths, environment variables, command arguments, and canary secrets are removed.
4. The redaction is deterministic and does not alter safe symbol names, line numbers, or signal metadata.
5. Unit tests cover Unix and Windows path formats, path separators, symlinks, Unicode usernames, and nested workspaces.
6. Integration tests exercise `check_previous_crash`, report writing, archival, and re-read behavior.
7. A regression fixture with canary paths and secrets proves that none escape to stored report files.
8. Any future crash upload path uses the same redacted representation and has an explicit opt-in/allowlist.
9. The issue includes before/after report samples with sensitive values replaced by placeholders.
10. Coverage for changed redaction/report code is at least 85%.
11. The issue #13 telemetry/privacy checklist is updated with the crash-report evidence.

## Non-goals

- Do not remove all symbolication detail.
- Do not upload crash reports by default.
- Do not treat obscuring a path in one display as sufficient if raw data is still persisted elsewhere.

title: feat(#5): move Runtime writes/deletes fail-closed, harden read sandbox
body:
Part of #5.

Extends the Runtime MCP adapter shipped in #1/#2 (route agent file reads through Simplicio Runtime; enforce Runtime reads for ACP sessions) to writes and deletes, and closes a real sandbox gap found while adding tests.

## What changed

- `SimplicioRuntimeFs::write_file`/`delete_file` now call the Runtime's `simplicio_fs_write`/`simplicio_fs_delete` tools instead of falling back to local disk (`LocalFs`). Read/write/delete now share one `with_client()` helper so all three fail closed identically: missing Runtime, incompatible Runtime, or a rejected call all block the operation with an actionable error, and none of them silently touch the filesystem directly.
- `relative_path()` gains a canonicalize-based symlink-escape check. The prior check was purely syntactic (`Path::components`), so a symlink placed *inside* the workspace root that resolves *outside* it (e.g. `root/link -> /etc/passwd`) normalized to an in-root-looking path and passed straight through to the Runtime — a real sandbox bypass. Both sides are now canonicalized and re-verified for containment when the target exists; a not-yet-existing target (e.g. a file about to be created) keeps the syntactic result, leaving the Runtime as the read authority for those paths (per `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`).
- `simplicio-runtime-client` already had (uncommitted, from an earlier pass at this same issue in this worktree) typed, capability-negotiated contracts for `search`/`list`/`stat`/`edit`/`exec` plus `read`/`write`/`delete` — each checked against the Runtime's `tools/list` response before sending, so an older/incompatible Runtime fails closed with a clear "capability X unavailable, required tool Y" error instead of a raw protocol error. `exec` is argv-only (no shell), rejects shell metacharacters before ever reaching the Runtime, and binary reads/writes go over base64. I found and fixed a real bug while adding tests: `secure_relative_path()` compared a canonicalized candidate against a *non*-canonicalized repo root, which rejected legitimate in-repo paths whenever the two representations disagreed (reproduced concretely on a Windows temp dir in this sandbox). Also added the missing `tempfile` dev-dependency so the crate's own test suite could build at all.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` updated to describe the new write/delete guarantee and to be explicit about what's implemented-but-unwired.

## Acceptance criteria from #5 — met vs. still open

Met, with evidence below:
- Read/write/delete for the agent filesystem go through Runtime MCP with no local fallback (was read-only before this PR).
- Runtime absent/incompatible blocks the operation with a clear error (capability negotiation + fail-closed `with_client`/`call_tool`).
- Sandbox blocks path traversal (regression, still passing) **and** symlink escape (new, actually verified — see test evidence).
- Same contract used by TUI/headless/workspace/ACP: both existing `SimplicioRuntimeFs::new(...)` call sites (`xai-grok-shell` ACP session spawn, `xai-grok-workspace` session context factory) are unaffected — same trait, same constructor, and both still compile (`cargo check`, see below).

Still open (not attempted here, scoping to keep this reviewable):
- **Write/delete transaction+rollback wiring for apply-patch**: the client's `edit()` method already sends `atomic: true, rollback: true` to a `simplicio_edit` tool, but no agent tool (e.g. `apply_patch`) calls it yet.
- **Search/list/stat migration**: `grep`/`grep_files`/`hashline_grep`/`list_dir` (in `xai-grok-tools/src/implementations/...`) still shell out to `ripgrep`/`tokio::fs` directly, completely bypassing `AsyncFileSystem` — the client-side `search`/`list`/`stat` contracts exist and are unit-tested but have zero consumers.
- **Exec migration**: the bash terminal backend still runs commands locally; `RuntimeClient::exec` exists (argv-only, shell-metachar rejection, capability-checked) but nothing calls it.
- **Full four-surface parity** for the above (once wired for one surface, still needs verifying across TUI/headless/workspace/ACP).
- **Metrics** (aggregated latency/savings, no user content) — not started.
- **Integration test against a fake/real MCP server** — the client's own unit tests mock at the JSON-parsing/path-validation layer; there's no fake `simplicio serve --mcp` stdio process exercising the full JSON-RPC round trip in this PR (the one real-server test is pre-existing and `#[ignore]`d, since no Runtime binary is installable in CI/this sandbox).
- **85% adapter coverage** — not measured; no coverage tooling was run.

## Validation

```
cargo test -p simplicio-runtime-client
# running 6 tests
# test tests::reads_a_real_file_through_runtime_mcp ... ignored, requires an installed Simplicio Runtime with MCP tools/list
# test tests::parses_runtime_capabilities_contract ... ok
# test tests::parses_runtime_file_read_contract_and_binary_payload ... ok
# test tests::rejects_shell_injection_before_mcp_request ... ok
# test tests::rejects_truncated_reads_instead_of_returning_partial_source ... ok
# test tests::secure_path_rejects_parent_traversal_and_external_absolute_paths ... ok
# test result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored

cargo test -p xai-grok-tools --lib computer::local::simplicio_runtime
# running 8 tests (all ok), including:
# rejects_symlink_escape_to_outside_target_windows ... ok
# rejects_symlink_escape_via_nested_directory_windows ... ok
# allows_symlink_that_stays_inside_workspace_windows ... ok
# write_file_fails_closed_without_local_fallback ... ok
# delete_file_fails_closed_without_local_fallback ... ok
# rejects_parent_escape ... ok  (regression: PR #1/#2 behavior unchanged)

cargo check -p xai-grok-workspace -p xai-grok-shell --lib   # clean
cargo fmt -p simplicio-runtime-client -p xai-grok-tools -- --check   # clean
git diff --check   # clean
```

The symlink-escape tests actually create a real symlink and verify the read/write path is denied — not a mocked assertion. Note: this repo's `xai-grok-tools-api` build has a pre-existing, unrelated bug where `xai-proto-build` hardcodes `/dev/stdout`/`/dev/null` as protoc arguments, which breaks native-Windows builds even with a valid `protoc.exe`; I worked around it locally (temp file + `NUL`) to get real test runs, then reverted that workaround since it's out of scope here — it's tracked separately.
