body:
Part of #10, #13, #14.

## #14 — docs: onboarding beta, arquitetura e runbooks operacionais

**Met, with evidence:**
- `docs/QUICKSTART.md` — PT+EN quickstart (prereqs, build, first run, Runtime/Mapper/fail-closed behavior, privacy controls, known beta limitations).
- `docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` — login, Runtime, mapa, rede, updater.
- `scripts/check_doc_links.py` — validates every relative Markdown link + anchor + `cargo ... -p <crate>` command referenced in docs. **Passes clean**: `checked 35 Markdown file(s), 21 external link(s) seen / all documentation links and referenced cargo commands are valid`. Along the way it caught (and this PR fixes) 4 genuinely broken pre-existing links: a stale English README nav anchor pointing at a Portuguese heading, and 3 wrong relative paths in the inherited hooks/dashboard docs (one pointed at a plan doc that doesn't exist in this public tree — removed rather than left dangling).
- README now links all the above plus `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` from a "Simplicio-specific docs" section.

**Not done / open:** ADRs, ordered runbooks (release/rollback/rotação/incidente) beyond what's in TROUBLESHOOTING.md, and a clean-environment onboarding test — out of scope for this slice, left for follow-up.

## #13 — privacy: telemetria mínima e controles de dados

The existing telemetry engine (`crates/codegen/xai-grok-telemetry`) turned out to already be fairly mature — mode gating, Sentry PII scrubbing, fail-closed OTel redaction/allowlisting. This PR adds the two concrete gaps called out in the issue:

- **`DO_NOT_TRACK` support** (community convention), wired into both the sync gates used before the async config is available (`is_telemetry_disabled_sync`/`is_telemetry_explicitly_disabled_sync`) and the async `Config::resolve_telemetry_mode` — satisfies "opt-out é respeitado antes do primeiro evento" for the earliest-initialized subsystems too, not just the happy path.
- **`simplicio-code privacy diagnose`** — a new CLI command (and pure `build_diagnostic_report()` function) that lists every telemetry/crash-report/trace destination and whether it's currently active, with **zero network calls**. Satisfies "usuário consegue listar o que seria enviado."
- `docs/privacy/telemetry.md` documents the full destination inventory, the never-sent-fields allowlist (backed by a real `NEVER_SENT_FIELDS` const, not just prose), and honestly flags what's *not* done (backend retention/deletion enforcement, an e2e network-capture proxy test, and Mixpanel removal — all out of scope for a client-side PR).

**Evidence:** `cargo test -p xai-grok-telemetry --lib` → **157 passed, 0 failed** (9 new: `diagnostic_report::tests::*` ×7, `config::tests::do_not_track_*` ×2). `cargo test -p xai-grok-shell --lib do_not_track` → 1 of 2 new tests passed (`do_not_track_overrides_explicit_enable_in_sync_gates`); the other could not be verified due to an unrelated pre-existing bug — see "Bugs found" below. `cargo check -p xai-grok-pager` → clean (`Finished dev profile ... in 3m 45s`, only pre-existing unrelated warnings).

## #10 — feat: sincronizar login e entitlement durante updates de versões legadas

Correctly still blocked: #3 (login/entitlement) and #4 (gateway) do not exist in this tree yet (confirmed — `simplicio-code login` here is still the pre-existing OAuth2/device-auth flow). Rather than build migration logic against a fictional API:

- Cataloged the **real** current config/session formats (`~/.grok/config.toml`, `auth.json`, session `.jsonl` files, no existing schema-version field) — step 1 of the issue.
- `docs/migration/legacy-login-migration.md` — design doc for the migration manifest format, idempotency/resumability, and rollback strategy, explicitly marked "design only, pending #3/#4."

## Bugs found in passing (flagged separately, not fixed in this PR — out of scope here)

- **`crates/build/xai-proto-build`** hardcodes `--dependency_out=/dev/stdout` / `--descriptor_set_out=/dev/null`, which breaks *every* Windows build/test even with a correctly installed `protoc` (verified `protoc --version` works standalone; the build script's literal `/dev/stdout` path fails on native Windows). This confirms the README's "Windows builds are best-effort" note is currently a hard blocker, not just untested. Worked around locally (temp files, not committed) to actually run `cargo test`/`cargo check` for this PR's verification.
- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-models/default_models.json`**'s validator compares the model's `id` against a list built from the `model` field instead of `id`, so it panics (`'default' is 'simplicio-1' but 'models' array only has ["tencent/hy3:free"]`) the first time the default model is resolved — including plain `Config::default()`. Looks **release-blocking** for the just-merged Simplicio-1 identity work (#17/PR #19-adjacent). This is why one of this PR's own new tests couldn't be verified end-to-end (documented honestly in `docs/privacy/telemetry.md` §6 rather than hidden).

Both are filed as separate follow-up tasks; neither is fixed inside this PR since they're unrelated to #10/#13/#14's scope.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo test -p xai-grok-telemetry --lib` — 157 passed, 0 failed
- [x] `python scripts/check_doc_links.py` — all documentation links and referenced cargo commands are valid
- [x] `cargo check -p xai-grok-pager` — clean, new `privacy diagnose` CLI wiring compiles
- [x] `cargo test -p xai-grok-shell --lib do_not_track` — 1/2 passed; 1 blocked by unrelated pre-existing bug (see above)

title: [bug] perf_gate cannot run because engine.simplicio_compress is absent from the repository
body:
## Context

The performance gate shipped with the repository is not executable from a clean checkout. It imports a package that is not present in the repository or on the default Python path.

## Reproduction

From the repository root on 2026-07-17:

```sh
python3 scripts/perf_gate.py --json
```

Observed failure:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../scripts/perf_gate.py", line 268, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File ".../scripts/perf_gate.py", line 187, in main
    report = run_benchmark(args.cycles)
  File ".../scripts/perf_gate.py", line 112, in run_benchmark
    total_ms, durations = _bench_compress(cycles)
  File ".../scripts/perf_gate.py", line 69, in _bench_compress
    from engine.simplicio_compress import compress
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'engine'
```

Repository evidence:

- `scripts/perf_gate.py` imports `engine.simplicio_compress`.
- There is no `engine/` package in the checkout.
- The repository contains `scripts/simplicio-engine`, but that launcher references a separate `engine/simplicio_engine.py` path that is also absent here.
- Related documentation under `.agents/`, `.claude/`, and `scripts/sync_plugin.py` describes `engine/` as an excluded/pip-only surface, which conflicts with a committed CI/performance gate requiring the import.

## Impact

- Issue #12 cannot obtain a reproducible performance report or enforce its regression gate.
- Any CI job invoking `perf_gate.py` fails before measuring latency, memory, or convergence.
- The repository cannot distinguish a real performance regression from a packaging/import-layout error.
- The current failure undermines the claimed benchmark evidence and can silently leave release gates unused if the script is skipped.

## Investigation steps

1. Determine whether the canonical engine belongs in this repository, in the Simplicio Loop package, or behind an explicit installed dependency.
2. Inspect the CI/install surfaces that invoke `perf_gate.py` and the expected working directory/PYTHONPATH.
3. Reproduce from a clean checkout and from the supported installed package.
4. Verify whether `scripts/simplicio-engine` is intended to launch the same implementation or is stale.
5. Define one canonical import/entrypoint and remove contradictory fallback assumptions.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `python3 scripts/perf_gate.py --json` runs successfully from a clean checkout using only documented dependencies, or fails with a structured actionable diagnostic if the benchmark is intentionally unavailable.
2. The benchmark imports the canonical compression engine without relying on an undeclared current-directory package.
3. CI invokes the same command and fails closed on benchmark errors rather than silently skipping the gate.
4. The baseline schema, p50/p95 latency, throughput, memory, and convergence fields are produced and validated.
5. A clean-install/system test exercises the exact supported installation path.
6. Unit tests cover import resolution and the missing-engine error path.
7. Benchmark tests remain deterministic and do not include private code, real prompts, or network calls.
8. Documentation identifies the owner/package/version of the engine dependency and the supported invocation.
9. Any change to the performance gate includes before/after benchmark output and a regression threshold test.
10. Coverage for new gate/import code is at least 85%.

## Non-goals

- Do not fabricate benchmark numbers when the engine is unavailable.
- Do not vendor an unrelated engine implementation without a clear ownership boundary.
- Do not disable issue #12's regression gate merely to make CI green.

title: [bug] installed E2E selftest references missing fully-measured events fixture
body:
## Context

The installed/isolated E2E harness cannot complete its own deterministic selftest because the fixture path hard-coded by the probe does not exist in the repository.

## Reproduction

From the repository root:

```sh
python3 scripts/check_e2e_installed.py selftest
```

Observed on 2026-07-17:

```
python:
  check_e2e_installed selftest: FAIL (1/3)
```

Direct decomposition of the selftest:

- `simplicio-mapper` shim probe: pass.
- `simplicio-dev-cli` shim probe: pass.
- `simplicio-loop` shim probe: pass.
- strict audit: fail because the events file is missing.
- probe command: exits 2 for the same reason.

Missing path:

```
contracts/e2e-demo/v1/fixtures/fully-measured/events.jsonl
```

The script defines this path as its canonical `FIXTURE_EVENTS`, but the file is absent from the checkout.

## Expected

The selftest must be offline, deterministic, and runnable from a clean checkout. It should either ship the complete fixture at the canonical path or explicitly construct an equivalent temporary fixture in the selftest.

## Impact

- The installed E2E gate fails even when all three required command shims/probes pass.
- CI cannot distinguish a missing test artifact from a real operator/runtime failure.
- Issue #11 quality gates and issue #12 benchmark/evidence surfaces cannot consume a stable E2E receipt.
- The harness currently has no passing baseline on a clean checkout.

## Investigation steps

1. Inspect the fixture contract and the strict event schema expected by `_audit_events`.
2. Compare `scripts/e2e_demo.py` event output with the fixture schema.
3. Check whether the fixture was intended to be generated, vendored, or restored from another repository.
4. Run the selftest from the repository root and from an arbitrary temporary consumer directory.
5. Validate the fixture with the same strict audit used in `probe`.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `python3 scripts/check_e2e_installed.py selftest` passes 3/3 from a clean checkout with no network and no external operators.
2. The fully-measured fixture exists under the documented path, is versioned, and validates against the strict event schema.
3. The fixture contains only synthetic/non-sensitive data and no prompts, source code, tokens, or real paths.
4. A second test proves the selftest is independent of the caller's current working directory.
5. A negative fixture proves simulated/stand-in hops are rejected.
6. `probe --events` and `run --require-measured` use the same schema/version and produce actionable failures for missing or malformed fixtures.
7. CI runs the selftest and archives the measured receipt/events on failure.
8. Documentation identifies the fixture owner, schema version, regeneration command, and review process.
9. Unit/integration/system/regression tests cover missing, malformed, incomplete, and fully measured fixtures.
10. Coverage for changed harness/audit code is at least 85%.

## Non-goals

- Do not weaken strict audit to accept an empty or simulated fixture.
- Do not fabricate a passing measured receipt.
- Do not make the selftest depend on network or the user's installed operator versions.

body:
## Context

Workspace verification is blocked because the host filesystem is at 100% capacity. The failure occurs before the requested package can compile.

## Reproduction

Repository: /Users/wesleysimplicio/Projetos/ai/simplicio-code
Date: 2026-07-17

Command:
R=/Users/wesleysimplicio/Projetos/ai/simplicio-runtime/target/debug/simplicio
R shell --repo /Users/wesleysimplicio/Projetos/ai/simplicio-code -- sh -c 'cargo test -p xai-grok-config --locked --no-fail-fast'

Observed:
error: failed to create directory /Users/wesleysimplicio/Projetos/ai/simplicio-code/target/debug/.fingerprint/xai-grok-config-2d40d9c691d6ce84
No space left on device (os error 28)

Host evidence:
/dev/disk3s5  460Gi  411Gi  116Mi  100%  /System/Volumes/Data

Largest measured directories, without mutation:
- simplicio-code/target: 26G
- ~/.cache: 8.5G
- ~/.cargo: 955M
- simplicio-code/.simplicio: 467M

## Impact

- Package/unit/integration tests cannot start reliably.
- Cargo can fail while writing fingerprints, artifacts, or incremental state.
- simplicio-loop quality gates cannot produce trustworthy evidence.
- Repeated retries may worsen disk pressure or leave partial build artifacts.

## Required remediation

1. Define a disk-budget policy for local and CI verification.
2. Add a preflight check that reports free bytes, required minimum, largest relevant caches, and a remediation command before build/test.
3. Ensure failed/interrupted builds do not leave unbounded incremental artifacts.
4. Document safe cleanup commands that preserve source changes, worktrees, leases, and receipts.

## Acceptance criteria

1. A preflight command reports free bytes, required minimum, largest relevant caches, and remediation before a build.
2. The quality gate returns structured disk_space_insufficient instead of a raw Cargo traceback.
3. Cleanup is scoped to disposable build/cache artifacts and never deletes source changes, untracked user files, worktrees, leases, or receipts.
4. Cargo target/cache budgets and retention policy are documented and enforced.
5. Tests cover sufficient space, insufficient space, boundary conditions, and interrupted builds.
6. CI exposes disk usage and fails with actionable artifacts when space is insufficient.
7. The issue includes before/after disk measurements and successful verification after remediation.
8. No cleanup runs automatically without explicit policy and an auditable receipt.

title: docs(rfc): design-stage response to #30 and #31 (evolution + adaptive architecture coordinators)
body:
Part of #30
Part of #31

## Why this is an RFC and not an implementation

Issues #30 and #31 ask for a full **Continuous Evolution Coordinator** and **Adaptive Architecture Coordinator** — schemas, cross-repo dedup/ownership resolution, DAG diffing, capacity/cost simulation, historical replay, shadow runs, canary rollout of new agent stages, and a self-approval-resistant approval matrix — to be built directly into the Simplicio Code coding-agent product. This describes a multi-month subsystem with real self-modifying-agent-topology safety implications (auto-approval matrices, silent architecture mutation, backlog explosion). It is not realistically buildable or safely verifiable in a single session, and fabricating an integrated "Definition of Done" run for either issue would be dishonest engineering theater, not real delivery.

**RFC/design response — full implementation is out of scope for a single session and carries real self-modifying-system safety risk that needs human architectural review before any code executes with mutation authority.**

## What this PR actually does

1. **`docs/rfc/continuous-evolution-coordinator.md`** (#30) — the `simplicio.evolution-proposal/v1` contract, taxonomy/creation-gate rules, an honest 6-phase rollout (phase 0-2 buildable now with no GitHub access; phases 3+ need org-level review), an explicit Code/`simplicio-loop` scope boundary (the loop already owns manifest/coordinator/receipt primitives per #466-468 — this proposes Code *emit* signals rather than duplicate a coordinator), and a safety-mitigation table (self-approval, backlog explosion, secret/PII leakage, prompt injection via untrusted issue content, cross-repo ownership mistakes).
2. **`docs/rfc/adaptive-architecture-coordinator.md`** (#31) — the `simplicio.topology-change/v1` contract, the full validation/simulation/approval/canary lifecycle from the issue, an 8-phase rollout, and hard safety invariants (proposer/coordinator never approves its own change, `weaken_gate`/`change_isolation` changes are always `critical` risk and never auto-promoted, active runs stay pinned to their manifest hash, no unbounded coordinator-creates-coordinator recursion).
3. **`scripts/evolution/fingerprint_dedup.py`** + **`scripts/tests/test_fingerprint_dedup.py`** — the one slice that is genuinely safe to build now: a standalone, additive Python module implementing fingerprint-based dedup and a non-authoritative taxonomy-hint classifier over the 8-class taxonomy from #30. It makes no GitHub API calls, has no network access, writes no files, is not imported by any existing binary/hook/CI job, and does not touch `Cargo.toml` or any Rust crate (per the issues' own invariant that the generated root `Cargo.toml` stays read-only). All 7 tests pass: `python3 scripts/tests/test_fingerprint_dedup.py`.
4. `.gitignore`: added `__pycache__/` / `*.pyc` (a real, pre-existing gap now that `scripts/` has more Python tooling).

Both RFCs explicitly list what is **not** implemented (GitHub dedup/search, issue creation, DAG differ, simulator, canary/kill-switch, approval-matrix enforcement, CLI surfaces, etc.) and end with open questions for human/architectural review before further phases proceed.

## Verification

- `python3 scripts/tests/test_fingerprint_dedup.py` — all 7 tests pass.
- `python3 scripts/check_doc_links.py` — all links/anchors/cargo-command references in the new docs resolve correctly (41 Markdown files checked, 0 failures).
- No Rust files changed; no `Cargo.toml`/workspace member changes.

## Test plan

- [x] New Python module's own test suite passes.
- [x] Repo's doc-link checker passes against the new RFC docs.
- [ ] Human/architectural review of the RFCs' open questions (see end of each doc) before any further phase is implemented.

title: fix(xai-grok-models): validate default_models.json 'default' against entry id, not model
body:
## Bug

`crates/codegen/xai-grok-models/src/lib.rs`'s startup validator for
`default_models.json` panicked on default-model resolution — release-blocking
for the just-merged Simplicio-1 identity work, since Simplicio-1 is now the
only entry in `default_models.json`.

Reproduced directly before any fix:

```
thread 'repro_test::repro_panic' panicked at crates\codegen\xai-grok-models\src\lib.rs:37:5:
default_models.json: 'default' is 'simplicio-1' but 'models' array only has ["tencent/hy3:free"]
```

## Root cause

`default_models.json` uses two distinct concepts per catalog entry:

- `id` — a stable, human-facing identifier (`"simplicio-1"`)
- `model` — the routing slug actually sent to the provider API (`"tencent/hy3:free"`)

The top-level `default` field (and `web_search` / `image_description` /
`session_summary`) is documented to name a catalog entry by its `id`, falling
back to `model` when an entry has no `id` — this is the exact key scheme used
everywhere the runtime catalog is actually built from this same file
(`build_prefetched_map` and `default_models()` in
`xai-grok-shell/src/agent/config.rs`, both computing
`m.id.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| m.model.clone())`).

The validator in `xai-grok-models` never learned this convention: its
`DefaultModelEntry` struct only deserialized `model`, and compared `default`
against the list of `model` slugs instead of the `id`-or-`model` key. Since
`default_models.json` sets `"default": "simplicio-1"` (an `id`) while the
entry's `model` is `"tencent/hy3:free"`, the assert always failed — for
*any* entry that sets `id`, which is the normal case and true of the sole
shipped Simplicio-1 entry.

This wasn't caught because the crate had zero tests — the check only runs
inside a `LazyLock`, so it silently never fired until something actually
called `default_model()` (or a friend) at runtime.

## Fix

`crates/codegen/xai-grok-models/src/lib.rs`:
- `DefaultModelEntry` now deserializes `id: Option<String>` in addition to
  `model`, with a `key()` helper: `id.as_deref().unwrap_or(&model)`.
- `validate()` builds the key set from `key()` (not raw `model` slugs) and
  checks `default`, `web_search`, `image_description`, and
  `session_summary` all resolve against it.

No change to `default_models.json` itself — the data was correct, the
validator was checking the wrong field.

## Tests added

- `real_shipped_json_resolves_default_models_without_panicking` — loads the
  actual shipped `default_models.json` (the file baked into the release
  binary) through every public accessor (`default_model`,
  `default_web_search_model`, `default_image_description_model`,
  `default_session_summary_model`). This is the exact case that used to
  panic.
- `real_shipped_json_is_internally_consistent` — asserts no duplicate keys
  across `models` entries and re-runs `validate()` against the real file.
- `key_prefers_id_over_model` / `key_falls_back_to_model_when_id_absent` —
  unit tests for the fallback helper.
- `validate_panics_when_default_references_model_slug_not_id` — reproduces
  the original bug in isolation (`should_panic`).
- `validate_accepts_default_matching_entry_id` — the fixed, passing case.

## Test evidence

Before (throwaway repro test calling `default_model()`, since the crate had
no tests to catch this):

```
running 1 test
test repro_test::repro_panic ... FAILED
thread 'repro_test::repro_panic' panicked at crates\codegen\xai-grok-models\src\lib.rs:37:5:
default_models.json: 'default' is 'simplicio-1' but 'models' array only has ["tencent/hy3:free"]
test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
```

After (`cargo test -p xai-grok-models`):

```
running 6 tests
test tests::key_falls_back_to_model_when_id_absent ... ok
test tests::key_prefers_id_over_model ... ok
test tests::real_shipped_json_is_internally_consistent ... ok
test tests::real_shipped_json_resolves_default_models_without_panicking ... ok
test tests::validate_accepts_default_matching_entry_id ... ok
test tests::validate_panics_when_default_references_model_slug_not_id - should panic ... ok

test result: ok. 6 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
```

`cargo fmt -p xai-grok-models` and `cargo clippy -p xai-grok-models --all-targets -- -D warnings` both clean.

Note: a full `cargo test --workspace` run wasn't possible in this sandbox —
`xai-grok-tools-api`'s build script needs a `protoc` binary that isn't
present/compatible in this environment (`bin/protoc` is committed for a
different platform: "%1 não é um aplicativo Win32 válido"). This is a
pre-existing sandbox limitation unrelated to this change; `xai-grok-models`
itself has no protobuf dependency and its own test suite is fully green.

title: xai-proto-build hardcoded Unix paths break native Windows builds
body:
## Summary

`crates/build/xai-proto-build/src/lib.rs` (`emit_rerun_if_changed`) hardcoded `/dev/stdout` and `/dev/null` as literal paths passed to `protoc --dependency_out=...` / `--descriptor_set_out=...`. These are Unix-only pseudo-paths that don't exist on Windows, so any native Windows build of a crate depending on this build script (e.g. `xai-grok-tools-api`, and transitively most of the workspace) fails — even with a working `protoc.exe` available via `$PROTOC`.

Discovered independently by multiple agents working the repo on 2026-07-17 as a recurring side-finding, not filed as its own issue until now.

## Fix

A fix for the hardcoded-path bug is proposed on branch `codex/fix-protoc-windows` (PR to follow): replace the Unix pseudo-paths with real temp files created via the already-vendored `tempfile` crate, and adjust the Makefile-dependency-rule parsing accordingly (splitting on the first `": "` rule separator instead of assuming a literal `/dev/null:` prefix, which is also safe against Windows drive-letter colons like `C:\...`).

## Residual/related gap: protoc resolution via DotSlash on Windows

Separately, `bin/protoc`'s DotSlash manifest was missing a `windows-x86_64` platform entry entirely (also fixed alongside the above, pinned to the same protoc v29.3 release as the other platforms). However, adding the manifest entry alone is *not* sufficient for `find_protoc.rs`'s `bin/protoc` walk-up-directories step to succeed natively on Windows: DotSlash files are `#!/usr/bin/env dotslash` shebang scripts, and Windows has no shebang support, so `Command::new("bin/protoc")` still fails with "not a valid Win32 application" (os error 193) unless one of the following is also wired up:

- `dotslash` is installed and this path is invoked through it explicitly, or
- a DotSlash Windows shim executable (see https://dotslash-cli.com/docs/windows/) is placed alongside as `bin/protoc.exe`, which `find_protoc.rs` does not currently search for.

Until either is implemented, Windows developers need to set `$PROTOC` or install `protoc` on `$PATH` to unblock local builds (steps 1/3 of `find_protoc`'s search order already support this).

## Verification

Reproduced and verified fixed on a native Windows 11 machine:
- Before: `cargo check -p xai-grok-tools-api` panics in `build.rs` (`protoc command failed` / previously would panic differently after the dotslash gap is also patched, since `/dev/null`/`/dev/stdout` don't exist).
- After (with `$PROTOC` pointed at a real `protoc.exe` v29.3): `cargo check -p xai-grok-tools-api` succeeds.

title: perf: warm-client-reuse + real N=3 repeated sample for Runtime bench (#12)
body:
Part of #12.

Extends the existing `simplicio-perf-bench` harness added in #20 (still open) — no rewrite. Closes two of that PR's explicitly-open gaps: no repeated statistical sample (was N=1) and no warm-client-reuse variant.

## What changed

- **`--repeats 3` real run** (previously the committed baseline was N=1). `LatencyStats` (p50/p95/mean/sample-stdev) already existed in `src/lib.rs` from #20 — this just runs with `repeats > 1` so those numbers are non-degenerate instead of single point estimates.
- **New `runtime_attempt_warm` path** in `src/bin/bench.rs`: spawns one `RuntimeClient` once before the golden-task loop and reuses it for every task/repeat's read, instead of the cold path's fresh process spawn + MCP `initialize`/`notifications/initialized` handshake per call. A new `warm_client_handshake` / `runtime_handshake` report entry records the one-time handshake cost separately.
- Refactored the cold-path scoring logic (success/content-match/notes) into a shared `build_runtime_task_result` helper so cold and warm are scored identically and comparably.
- The bytes/4 `approx_tokens` heuristic already existed from #20 and is unchanged — confirmed it still populates correctly.
- Appended (did not rewrite) `docs/perf/token-latency-benchmark.md` with a new "Update 2026-07-17: repeated sample (N=3) + warm-client-reuse variant" section, and updated "What remains open" to reflect what's now measured vs still blocked.
- New baseline file `crates/codegen/simplicio-perf-bench/baselines/run_2026-07-17-repeats3-warmcold.json` (kept the original N=1 baseline file untouched, per "append don't fabricate historical trend").

## Real numbers measured this run

Release build, Windows, Simplicio Runtime CLI 3.5.2 resolved on `PATH`, `--repeats 3`:

| Task | Path | p50 (ms) | p95 (ms) | stdev (ms) | success_rate |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| `read_small` | `direct_read` | 0.23 | 16.43 | 9.35 | 1.0 |
| `read_small` | `runtime_attempt` (cold) | 22,186 | 26,101 | 2,391 | 0.0 |
| `read_small` | `runtime_attempt_warm` | 0.0000 | 0.0005 | 0.00029 | 0.0 |
| `read_medium` | `runtime_attempt` (cold) | 14,355 | 23,339 | 5,199 | 0.0 |
| `read_medium` | `runtime_attempt_warm` | 0.0001 | 0.0003 | 0.00012 | 0.0 |
| `read_large_synthetic` (4MiB) | `runtime_attempt` (cold) | 28,324 | 29,921 | 1,050 | 0.0 |
| `read_large_synthetic` (4MiB) | `runtime_attempt_warm` | 0.0001 | 0.0004 | 0.00017 | 0.0 |

Full table (all 5 tasks) in the doc update. One-time `warm_client_handshake`: **31.80s**, failed.

## Honest caveats (please read before trusting these numbers)

- **N=3, not statistically robust.** Cold-path stdev across only 3 repeats is itself 1-5 *seconds* — the run-to-run variance is on the same order as the signal. N>=20 is still needed for a trustworthy p50/p95.
- **The Runtime MCP handshake still fails on this machine** with the same malformed-JSON error as the original #20 run (`expected ',' or ']' at line 1 column 10181`, tracked as Runtime #3298). So:
  - Warm reuse cuts *measured* per-call latency by ~5 orders of magnitude (tens of seconds → sub-millisecond) once the one-time handshake is paid — but that's because the *cached failure* returns instantly, not because a Runtime read succeeded quickly. This is a real, measured demonstration of the warm-reuse mechanism's overhead savings, not a validated warm-vs-cold comparison of successful reads.
  - No `runtime_attempt`/`runtime_attempt_warm` cell in any run to date (N=1 or N=3) has ever returned comparable content, so `approx_tokens` and `content_matches_direct` remain `null` for the Runtime path in this update too — there is nothing new to report on Runtime-side token counts.
- Root-causing the MCP capability gap, and a true CI-reproducible Runtime/Mapper comparison, remain blocked on #5/#6 as before.

- [x] `cargo test -p simplicio-perf-bench --lib` — 7/7 passing

title: fix(privacy): redact crash-report paths (#25); disk-budget preflight (#32)
body:
## Summary

Closes #25
Part of #32

Also investigated #28 and #29 — both are **blocked** by a premise mismatch (details below); no fix is included for them in this PR.

### #25 — redact absolute source paths from crash reports (CLOSES)

`symbolicate::resolve_frames` kept the raw, absolute filename from debug-info symbolication (`sym.filename().map(|f| f.display().to_string())`), and `check_previous_crash` wrote that text verbatim to `last-crash-report.txt` and archived it under `history/`.

Fix:
- New `crates/codegen/xai-crash-handler/src/redact.rs` module, documented, with a single choke point `redact_report(text: &str) -> String`.
- Wired into `symbolicate::format_report` (the one place that builds report text for the public API), so `check_previous_crash` gets it automatically — no separate call site to forget.
- Strips: Windows drive-letter paths, UNC paths, Unix absolute paths (keeping a relative tail anchored at `src/`, `crates/`, `tests/`, `examples/`, `benches/`, `bin/` so file/line context stays useful), `KEY=value`-style env assignments, `Args:`/`Cmd:`/`Command:`-labelled lines, and known secret/token shapes (vendor API-key prefixes, `Bearer` tokens, a generic `canary_*` convention).
- Unit tests: Windows and Unix path forms, UNC paths, Unicode usernames (both path styles), nested workspace checkouts, cargo-registry paths, bare home-directory-only paths (no anchor — verified the username doesn't leak as a bogus "filename"), env vars, command-arg lines, known secret prefixes, Bearer tokens, and idempotence on already-relative/non-sensitive text.
- New cross-platform integration test (`tests/redaction_integration.rs`, no `#[cfg(unix)]` needed) that builds a synthetic, well-formed `last-crash.bin` from a real captured call stack (`backtrace::trace`), so symbolication resolves to a genuine, machine-local absolute path (this machine's actual username), then proves the file written by `check_previous_crash` and the one archived under `history/` never contain that raw absolute path.

Evidence (`cargo test -p xai-crash-handler --locked --no-fail-fast`, run on Windows in this repo):

```
running 26 tests
test redact::tests::... (16 tests) ... ok
test format::tests::... (3 tests) ... ok
test symbolicate::tests::... (2 tests) ... ok
test terminal::tests::... (3 tests) ... ok
test tests::check_previous_crash_returns_none_when_no_file ... ok
test result: ok. 26 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

Running tests/redaction_integration.rs
test check_previous_crash_never_writes_raw_absolute_path_from_synthetic_fixture ... ok
(stderr: "verified redaction against a real resolved absolute path from this machine")
test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed

Doc-tests xai_crash_handler: 1 passed
```

`tests/integration.rs` (the existing signal-raising suite) is `#![cfg(unix)]`-gated and reports 0 runnable tests on this Windows machine — that is pre-existing and unrelated to this change; it is expected to run on Linux/macOS CI.

Note: `cargo clippy -p xai-crash-handler --all-targets -- -D warnings` fails on a pre-existing, unrelated issue in `handler.rs:615` (`exception_code as i32` — an unnecessary-cast lint on Windows-only exception-handling code not touched here; confirmed via `git diff --stat` showing no changes to that file). Flagging for visibility, not fixing here to keep this PR scoped.

### #32 — disk-space preflight check (partial — the actionable, code-side ask)

Added `scripts/check_disk_budget.py`: reports free bytes, a configurable required minimum (default 5 GiB), the size of `target/`, `~/.cargo`, and `.simplicio/`, and a remediation string naming the largest offender — returns a structured `simplicio.disk-budget/v1` result with `status=disk_space_insufficient` (exit code 2) instead of a raw cargo OS error. Never deletes anything — this is preflight reporting only, per the issue's explicit "no automatic cleanup" requirement. Not wired into any existing command; it is a standalone, optional step (documented in its own module docstring) that CI or a future `perf_gate.py` can invoke and check the exit code / JSON `status` field of.

Evidence (`python3 scripts/tests/test_check_disk_budget.py`):

```
selftest: PASS (32/32)
```

Covers sufficient/insufficient/exact-boundary/one-byte-short decision logic (all via mocked `shutil.disk_usage` and mocked directory sizing — no real disk is filled), remediation text correctness (names the largest cache, never suggests `rm -rf`), a source-scan assertion that the module contains no deletion calls, and a CLI smoke test (both a trivially-satisfiable and an unsatisfiable `--min-free-gb`).

This machine's actual disk is not full, so the "real 100%-full disk" scenario from the issue's reproduction is inherently a local/external condition that cannot be reproduced directly (as the issue itself acknowledges) — the sufficient/insufficient logic is instead proven via the mocked unit tests above. Issue #32 also asks for "cargo target/cache budgets and retention policy" documentation and CI wiring to expose disk usage — those are left open as follow-up since they involve policy decisions (retention windows, what CI should assert) beyond a preflight reporting tool.

### #28 and #29 — blocked (premise mismatch, documented honestly rather than fabricated)

Both issues' reproduction steps reference scripts that do not exist anywhere in this repository's history:

```
$ python3 scripts/perf_gate.py --json
python3: cannot open file .../scripts/perf_gate.py: No such file or directory

$ python3 scripts/check_e2e_installed.py selftest
python3: cannot open file .../scripts/check_e2e_installed.py: No such file or directory
```

Confirmed via:

```
$ git show origin/main:scripts/perf_gate.py
fatal: path scripts/perf_gate.py does not exist in origin/main

$ git log --all --oneline -- scripts/perf_gate.py scripts/check_e2e_installed.py scripts/e2e_demo.py contracts/e2e-demo
(no output -- never existed in any local/origin ref)

$ grep -rl FIXTURE_EVENTS _audit_events simplicio_compress check_e2e_installed perf_gate . --include=*.py
(no output)
```

Both issues carry a maintainer comment ("Partial fix validated ... `python3 scripts/tests/test_perf_gate_unavailable.py` PASS") referencing files (`scripts/perf_gate.py`, `scripts/tests/test_perf_gate_unavailable.py`) that likewise do not exist in this repo or any branch/commit found — worth a maintainer sanity-check on whether that prior work actually landed anywhere, since as filed these issues cannot be reproduced against this checkout. Fixing #29 as scoped (just add the missing fixture) would require first building the entire `scripts/e2e_demo.py` / `check_e2e_installed.py` harness and `contracts/e2e-demo/v1/` schema from scratch — a much larger, riskier undertaking than the issue implies, and one that was not fabricated without a real spec to validate the fixture against. Recommend re-scoping or re-filing once the underlying harness exists in this repo.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p xai-crash-handler --locked --no-fail-fast` — 26 unit + 1 doctest + 1 new integration test, all passing
- [x] `python3 scripts/tests/test_check_disk_budget.py` — 32/32 checks passing
- [x] `cargo fmt -p xai-crash-handler -- --check` — clean
- [x] Manual CLI run of `scripts/check_disk_budget.py` against this real workspace
- [ ] `cargo clippy -p xai-crash-handler --all-targets -- -D warnings` — fails on a pre-existing, unrelated Windows-only lint in `handler.rs` (not introduced by this PR)

title: [bug] RuntimeClient gives no actionable diagnostic when Runtime MCP handshake fails
body:
## Context

The perf benchmark added for issue #12 (PR #20, #36) discovered that the real, installed Simplicio Runtime CLI (v3.5.2) fails its MCP handshake on this machine with a raw parse error ("invalid JSON response (JSON delimiter error at line 1 column 10181)"), tracked server-side at simplicio-runtime#3319 (and possibly related to the broader simplicio-runtime#3298). That's a Runtime-side bug, but it exposed a real client-side gap in `crates/codegen/simplicio-runtime-client`: when the handshake fails, callers get a multi-second hang (cold attempts took 2.9–29s before failing, with huge variance) and then a generic, low-signal error, instead of a fast, clear, actionable diagnostic.

## Why this matters beyond the current bug

Even once simplicio-runtime#3319 is fixed, the client will eventually meet OTHER Runtime failure modes (wrong version, corrupted install, network/IPC hiccup, Runtime crash mid-handshake) — issue #5's stated acceptance criteria explicitly require: *"Runtime ausente ou incompatível bloqueia a operação com instrução clara"* (Runtime absent or incompatible blocks the operation with a clear instruction). Today it does not meet that bar for a malformed-response failure mode — only for the already-handled "binary not found" (`RuntimeNotFound`) case.

## Proposed better solution (not just a bugfix)

1. Add a short, bounded timeout specifically for the `initialize` handshake step (distinct from the existing overall call timeout) so a broken handshake fails in ~1-2s, not 30s+.
2. When the handshake response fails to parse as JSON-RPC, capture and surface a bounded prefix of the raw bytes received (e.g. first 200 bytes, redacted of anything resembling a path/secret) in the error, so a user/developer immediately sees "got non-JSON-RPC output from Runtime, first bytes: ..." instead of a bare parse error.
3. Add a `simplicio-code`-side capability/version check right after a successful handshake (if one ever succeeds) so a version-incompatible Runtime is rejected with a clear message rather than causing confusing downstream tool-call failures — this is called out as required by issue #5 ("capability negotiation e erro de versão incompatível") but not yet implemented for the failure-to-parse case specifically.
4. Add a regression test using a fake "misbehaving" MCP server (one that emits a banner before JSON-RPC, or malformed JSON) proving the client fails fast with the new diagnostic, without needing a real broken Runtime binary to reproduce.

## Acceptance criteria

- A broken/malformed Runtime MCP response fails within ~1-2s, not the current multi-second-to-30s range.
- The resulting error message includes a bounded, redacted snippet of what was actually received, not just "invalid JSON response."
- A fake malformed-server test exists and passes, independent of any real installed Runtime binary.
- This client-side hardening should NOT paper over or mask the real server bug (simplicio-runtime#3319) — it's a UX/diagnostics improvement for this and future failure modes, not a workaround.

## Related

- simplicio-runtime#3319 (the actual server-side parse bug that surfaced this gap)
- simplicio-runtime#3298 (broader Runtime command-reliability catalog)
- #5 (Runtime migration — capability negotiation / clear-error acceptance criteria)
- #12, PR #20, PR #36 (benchmark that surfaced this)

title: security(#9): redact Debug output on secret-holding structs (SamplerConfig + others)
body:
Part of #9.

## Context

PR #23 (merged) fixed the raw `api_key`-logging leak in `xai-grok-sampler` and confirmed the `xai-grok-secrets` redaction pipeline is genuinely wired into OTel/telemetry/Sentry. It explicitly left one landmine open:

> `SamplerConfig` derives `Debug` with a plain `api_key: Option<String>` field — a latent risk (would leak the raw key if anything ever does `{:?}` on it) though no current call site does. Not fixed here to keep this PR scoped; flagged as a follow-up task.

This PR closes that landmine, and generalizes the audit to the rest of the workspace.

## What's fixed

Confirmed (reproduced, then fixed) — derived `Debug` replaced with a manual, redacting `Debug` impl, each with a regression test that constructs the struct with a canary secret, asserts `format!("{:?}", x)` never contains the canary, and asserts a `"<redacted>"` marker is present instead:

- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-sampler/src/config.rs`** — `SamplerConfig::api_key`, plus `extra_headers` (the session injects proxy-auth/bearer headers here per the field's own doc comment, so header *values* are redacted too, not just keys).
- **`crates/codegen/xai-chat-state/src/types.rs`** — `Credentials::api_key`/`alpha_test_key`. This one is transitive: `ChatStateSnapshot` embeds `Credentials` and also derives `Debug`, so `{:?}` on a snapshot (used throughout the actor) leaked the key too — fixing `Credentials`'s `Debug` fixes both.
- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-auth/src/auth_provider.rs`** — `CredentialSnapshot::token` (the actual bearer sent on the wire, used by OTel/401-attribution telemetry).
- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-mcp/src/oauth_config.rs`** — `McpOAuthConfig::client_secret` (`client_id` is left visible — it's a public OAuth identifier, not a secret).
- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-shell/src/agent/server.rs`** — `ServerConfig::secret` (the WS client-auth token) and `WsQueryParams::server_key` (the same token echoed from the query string).
- **`crates/codegen/simplicio-account-client/src/schema.rs`** — `TokenResponse::{access_token,refresh_token}`, `RefreshRequest::refresh_token`, `RevokeRequest::token`: the OAuth device-flow exchange types. `TokenResponse` in particular wasn't flagged by the original issue at all but is arguably the highest-value fix here — it's the literal parsed response from the token endpoint.

Defense-in-depth (no confirmed live call site prints them today, fixed anyway since they're one-line changes on the same pattern):
- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-pager/src/app/cli.rs`** — `ServeArgs::secret` (`--secret` / `GROK_AGENT_SECRET`).
- **`crates/codegen/xai-grok-shell/src/bin/trace_classify.rs`** — the dev-only replay tool's `Cli::api_key`.

## How these were found

Searched the workspace for `#[derive(...Debug...)]` on structs with fields named/shaped like `api_key`/`token`/`secret`/`password`/`authorization`/`bearer` whose type is a plain `String`/`Option<String>` (not already wrapped in a redacting newtype). Explicitly ruled out false positives: `xai-file-utils::S3StaticCredentials` (already has a hand-written redacting `Debug`), several fields that hold header/env-var *names* rather than secret *values*, and test-only structs with no `derive(Debug)` at all.

## Verification

This dev machine can't build the full workspace: `xai-grok-tools-api`'s `build.rs` requires `protoc` and hardcodes `--dependency_out=/dev/stdout --descriptor_set_out=/dev/null` (POSIX-only paths) — a pre-existing Windows-only build gap PR #23 already discovered and flagged as a follow-up, unrelated to and untouched by this change. Most of the fixed crates depend on `xai-grok-tools-api` transitively and are blocked by this on Windows.

What I could verify directly:
- **Standalone repro**: built a minimal throwaway crate mirroring `SamplerConfig`'s exact field shapes and the exact `Debug` impl/test added here, compiled with real `rustc`/`cargo test`. It has two tests: one proving a *derived*-Debug twin struct genuinely leaks the raw key (confirming the original bug was real, not theoretical), and one proving the fixed manual `Debug` impl does not. Both pass.
- **Two of the eight fixed crates don't depend on `xai-grok-tools-api`** and built + tested for real on this machine:
  - `cargo test -p xai-grok-auth` — 1/1 pass (`credential_snapshot_debug_never_prints_raw_token`)
  - `cargo test -p simplicio-account-client` — 11/11 pass (includes the new `oauth_secret_bearing_types_never_print_raw_tokens_in_debug`, which also re-asserts serialization still round-trips the real token — only `Debug` is redacted, not `Serialize`)
  - `cargo clippy -p xai-grok-auth -p simplicio-account-client --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — clean on every file this PR touches (verified via a targeted grep of the full-workspace fmt-check output against just these 8 paths — zero matches).
- `gitleaks detect --no-git -c .gitleaks.toml` (v8.30.1, downloaded fresh for this environment) against the full working tree: 1 finding, pre-existing/unrelated (`scripts/tests/test_precommit_secret_gate.py`, an already-`//gitleaks:allow`-annotated AWS-key-shaped fixture). None of the new canary secret strings this PR's tests introduce trip it.
- The remaining six fixed crates (`xai-grok-sampler`, `xai-chat-state`, `xai-grok-mcp`, `xai-grok-shell` x2, `xai-grok-pager`) are syntactically identical in pattern to the two verified above (`f.debug_struct(...).field("x", &opt.as_ref().map(|_| "<redacted>"))...finish()`) but couldn't be compiled locally due to the protoc gap above — expected to build in CI where `protoc` is available.

## What's still open

- The Windows `protoc`/`/dev/stdout` build gap itself (pre-existing, tracked by PR #23, not attempted here — fixing a build script for a platform this environment can't even test against would be unverifiable).
- Centralized secret manager: still out of scope per the issue's own framing (server-side infra, doesn't exist in this client-side repo).
- This is a targeted sweep, not an exhaustive one — I focused on structs with a plausible real call site (config objects passed through logging/tracing layers, parsed HTTP response bodies) over pure CLI-arg structs, though I fixed two of the latter anyway since they were cheap.

## Test plan

- [x] Standalone rustc/cargo repro proving the original `SamplerConfig`-shaped bug was real, then proving the fix — 2/2 pass
- [x] `cargo test -p xai-grok-auth` — 1/1 pass
- [x] `cargo test -p simplicio-account-client` — 11/11 pass
- [x] `cargo clippy -p xai-grok-auth -p simplicio-account-client --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — clean on all touched files
- [x] `gitleaks detect --no-git` — 1 pre-existing/unrelated finding, no new canaries trip it
- [ ] `cargo test -p xai-grok-sampler -p xai-chat-state -p xai-grok-mcp -p xai-grok-shell -p xai-grok-pager` — blocked locally by the pre-existing Windows protoc gap; expected to pass in CI (ubuntu/macos jobs)

title: fix(xai-proto-build): use portable temp files instead of /dev/stdout and /dev/null
body:
## Summary

- `crates/build/xai-proto-build/src/lib.rs` hardcoded `--dependency_out=/dev/stdout` and `--descriptor_set_out=/dev/null` when shelling out to `protoc`. These are Unix-only pseudo-paths that don't exist on Windows, so this build script — and therefore every crate depending on it (`xai-grok-tools-api`, and transitively most of the workspace) — failed to build natively on Windows, even with a working `protoc.exe` reachable via `$PROTOC`.
- Fixed by writing to real files inside a fresh `tempfile::TempDir` instead (the `tempfile` crate was already a dependency of this build-script crate), and updating the Makefile-dependency-rule parsing to split on the first `": "` rule separator rather than assume a literal `/dev/null:` prefix — this is also safe against Windows drive-letter colons (e.g. `C:\...`).
- Also added the missing `windows-x86_64` platform entry to `bin/protoc`'s DotSlash manifest (pinned to the same protoc v29.3 release used by the other platforms; hash/size verified against the real GitHub release asset).
- Documented in `find_protoc.rs` the residual gap: even with the manifest entry, `bin/protoc` still can't be executed natively on Windows without a DotSlash Windows shim binary (it's a `#!/usr/bin/env dotslash` shebang script, and Windows has no shebang support) — `$PROTOC` or a `protoc` on `$PATH` remain the supported Windows workarounds until that shim is added. Tracked in #35.

Resolves the cross-cutting Windows build blocker described in #35.

## Test plan

Verified on a native Windows 11 machine (this is not a claim from CI logs — actually run locally):

**Before** (this branch's parent, unmodified `xai-proto-build`), with a real `protoc.exe` v29.3 available via `$PROTOC`:
```
$ cargo check -p xai-grok-tools-api
...
thread 'main' (13120) panicked at crates\codegen\xai-grok-tools-api\build.rs:33:10:
called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: protoc command failed
Caused by:
    program not found
```
(`protoc` rejected `/dev/stdout` / `/dev/null` as invalid paths on Windows.)

**After** (this branch), same environment:
```
$ cargo check -p xai-grok-tools-api
   Compiling xai-proto-build v0.0.0 (...)
   Compiling xai-grok-tools-api v0.1.220-alpha.4 (...)
    Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 55.98s
```

Also ran:
- [x] `cargo check -p xai-proto-build` — clean
- [x] `cargo clippy -p xai-proto-build --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo fmt -p xai-proto-build -- --check` — clean
- [x] `cargo check --workspace` — kicked off; workspace is large (100+ crates) and didn't finish within the session, but progressed through dozens of downstream crates (including several `xai-grok-*` codegen crates) with no new errors introduced, only pre-existing unrelated warnings in unrelated files.
- [ ] Full `cargo check --workspace` to completion — not exhaustively confirmed; the specific broken crates (`xai-proto-build`, `xai-grok-tools-api`) are fully verified.

## Still open

- The DotSlash Windows-shim gap for natively resolving `bin/protoc` (documented in `find_protoc.rs` and tracked in #35) is not fixed in this PR — `$PROTOC`/`$PATH` remain the workaround.

title: feat(#5): wire search through the Runtime MCP client (grep_files consumer)
body:
Part of #5.

Continues the Runtime MCP wiring from #26 (which shipped fail-closed
read/write/delete via `SimplicioRuntimeFs` and typed but *unconsumed*
`search`/`list`/`stat`/`edit`/`exec` client contracts). This PR wires
`search` end-to-end through a real agent tool, following the same
fail-closed pattern as read/write/delete.

## What changed

- **`simplicio-runtime-client`**: hardened `RuntimeClient::search()`:
  - Optional repo-relative `path` scope now goes through `secure_relative_path`
    (same sandbox as read/write/delete/list/stat).
  - New `secure_glob()` rejects parent-traversal (`../`) and absolute globs
    *before* they reach the Runtime — previously `globs` were forwarded to the
    Runtime completely unchecked, the same class of bypass PR #26 fixed for
    symlinked read/write/delete targets.
  - Replaced the raw `serde_json::Value` return with a typed
    `simplicio.search-result/v1` contract (`SearchResult`/`SearchMatch`).
  - Added a fake stdio MCP test server (`tests/support/fake_server_main.rs`,
    built as a `[[bin]]` test fixture) and an integration test
    (`tests/fake_server_search.rs`) that spawns a real `RuntimeClient` process
    against it — full `initialize`/`tools/list`/`tools/call` round trip for
    `search`, plus a regression check that `read`/`write`/`delete` still work
    and still fail closed on path escape through the same client.

- **`xai-grok-tools`**: added an `AsyncSearch` trait (parallel to
  `AsyncFileSystem`) and implemented it on `SimplicioRuntimeFs`, sharing the
  same fail-closed `with_runtime` core and `relative_path` symlink-escape
  sandbox that read/write/delete already use. New tests: fails closed with no
  Runtime present, rejects a search scope that's a symlink escaping the
  workspace, rejects a plain `../` traversal scope.

- Wired an optional `SearchBackend` resource through `SessionContext` /
  `AgentBuilder` / `AgentRebuildSpec` at the two real Runtime-backed
  construction sites (ACP shell session spawn, workspace session context
  factory) — mirroring how `SimplicioRuntimeFs` itself is opted into only
  those two sites rather than replacing every `LocalFs` call site. Both sites
  now share **one** `SimplicioRuntimeFs` instance as both `AsyncFileSystem`
  and `AsyncSearch` trait objects, so read/write/delete/search reuse a single
  Runtime MCP session instead of each spawning its own subprocess.

- **First real consumer**: `CodexGrepFilesTool` (`grep_files`) checks for a
  `SearchBackend` resource. If present, it's used exclusively and its errors
  are surfaced directly (`"Simplicio Runtime search failed: ..."`) — **no
  fallback to local `rg`**, even if `rg` is installed and would find real
  matches. If absent (every other call site today), behavior is byte-for-byte
  unchanged. New tests cover: using the backend when present, deduplicating
  matches into a file list, and — the key regression — that a backend error
  does *not* silently fall back to local `rg` even when `rg` would have found
  a real match on disk.

- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` updated to describe the new search wiring and what's
  still open.

## What's still open for #5

- `list`/`stat`/`edit`/`exec` still have zero agent-tool consumers.
- `grep` (grok-build namespace), `hashline_grep`, and `list_dir` still shell
  out to ripgrep/`tokio::fs` directly — only the simpler `grep_files`
  (Codex namespace, files-with-matches only, no streaming) was wired in this
  slice, chosen because the grok-build `GrepTool` has substantial
  streaming/byte-cap/timeout machinery that would need careful redesign to
  preserve while going through a single-response MCP call; that's future work.
- `bash`/terminal execution still runs locally; `exec` is unwired.

## What I verified

- `cargo test -p simplicio-runtime-client`: **32 passed, 1 ignored, 0

fix(#38): fast-fail with actionable diagnostic on broken Runtime MCP handshake
body:
## Summary

Closes #38.

`crates/codegen/simplicio-runtime-client`'s `RuntimeClient::spawn_in` handshake (`initialize` + `tools/list`) previously had no bound on how long it would wait for a response, so a broken/slow/malformed Runtime MCP handshake could hang for 2.9-29s (per the issue, surfaced by the perf benchmark from #12/#20/#36 against a real Runtime hitting simplicio-runtime#3319) before failing with a bare, low-signal `serde_json` parse error.

This PR only hardens the **client's** handling of a broken handshake response — it does not attempt to fix the real server-side bug (tracked separately at simplicio-runtime#3319).

Changes in `crates/codegen/simplicio-runtime-client/src/lib.rs`:

- **Dedicated stdout reader thread**: `spawn_stdout_reader` moves blocking `ChildStdout` reads onto a background thread that forwards each raw line over an `mpsc` channel. This lets the main thread bound how long it waits for a response via `Receiver::recv_timeout`, which isn't portably possible directly on a blocking `BufRead::read_line` over a pipe.
- **`HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT` (2s)**: applied specifically to the `initialize` and `tools/list` startup calls in `spawn_in` (distinct from any later per-call timeout, e.g. `exec`'s `timeout_ms`). A hung handshake now fails with the new `Error::HandshakeTimeout { method, timeout }` instead of blocking.
- **Redacted response snippet on parse failure**: when a handshake response fails to parse as JSON-RPC, `Error::InvalidResponse` now includes a bounded (200-byte), redacted snippet of the raw bytes actually received, via a small local `redact_snippet`/`looks_like_absolute_path` helper (masks Windows drive-letter/UNC and Unix absolute paths). Deliberately does not pull in `xai-crash-handler`'s full regex-based crash-report redactor (env vars, secret prefixes, `Bearer` tokens, `Args:` lines) — that's a different crate, not a dependency here, and is more machinery than a 200-byte diagnostic snippet needs.
- **New regression test** `tests/handshake_diagnostics.rs`, driven against a fake misbehaving MCP server (`FAKE_RUNTIME_MODE=malformed_handshake` / `hangs_forever` added to the existing `tests/support/fake_server_main.rs` fake-Runtime binary — no real installed Runtime or network needed):
  - `malformed_handshake`: fake server replies to `initialize` with a plain-text banner instead of JSON-RPC. Asserts the client fails in well under 3s and that the error message contains a snippet of what was received, with the absolute path (and the username inside it) redacted.
  - `hangs_forever`: fake server never responds and never exits. Asserts the client fails via `Error::HandshakeTimeout` in ~2s, not 30s.
- Unit tests for `redact_snippet` covering Unix, Windows, and UNC absolute paths, plus truncation.

## Real test output

```
cargo test -p simplicio-runtime-client
...
running 36 tests
...
test result: ok. 35 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.06s

     Running tests\fake_server_search.rs
running 1 test
test fake_runtime_round_trips_search_and_keeps_read_write_delete_fail_closed_contract ... ok
test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out

     Running tests\handshake_diagnostics.rs
running 1 test
test fails_fast_with_redacted_snippet_on_malformed_handshake_and_on_a_hung_handshake ... ok
test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 2.14s
```

Before/after timing (captured via a temporary `eprintln!`, since removed, in the new test):

```
[timing] malformed_handshake elapsed = 69.2204ms   // fails fast on parse error, vs. previously succeeding-then-failing after a full read
[timing] hangs_forever elapsed = 2.0337464s        // bounded by HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT, vs. previously unbounded (2.9-29s+ against the real bug, or indefinite for a true hang)
```

The pre-existing single fake-server test (`tests/fake_server_search.rs`) and all lib unit tests pass unchanged, confirming no regression to the Runtime-not-found or successful-handshake paths.

`cargo clippy -p simplicio-runtime-client --all-targets` shows the same 6 pre-existing `disallowed_methods` (`Path::canonicalize` vs `dunce::canonicalize`) warnings as on `main` (verified via `git stash`) — no new warnings introduced.

## What remains open

- Item 3 from the issue ("simplicio-code-side capability/version check right after a successful handshake... a version-incompatible Runtime is rejected with a clear message") is **not** addressed here. This PR focuses on the fail-fast + diagnostic-snippet acceptance criteria (items 1, 2, 4); version/capability-mismatch handling for a *successful* handshake with an incompatible server is a separate concern better scoped with issue #5's broader capability-negotiation work. Flagging this as a gap rather than silently dropping it.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build -p simplicio-runtime-client`
- [x] `cargo test -p simplicio-runtime-client` (all previously-passing tests still pass, plus the new handshake diagnostics test and redact_snippet unit tests)
- [x] `cargo clippy -p simplicio-runtime-client --all-targets` (no new warnings vs. `main`)

title: fix(#38): fail-fast Runtime handshake timeout + redacted diagnostic
body:
## Summary

Closes #38

`RuntimeClient::spawn_in` previously ran `initialize`/`tools/list` through the
generic `request()` path with no bound of its own, so a broken/misbehaving
Runtime handshake could hang for the full multi-second-to-30s range observed
in #38 before returning a bare `invalid Simplicio Runtime response: ...`
error with no indication of what was actually received.

Changes (`crates/codegen/simplicio-runtime-client/src/lib.rs`):

- `DEFAULT_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` (2s), distinct from the existing
  `DEFAULT_EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS` used by ordinary tool calls.
- `request_with_timeout`: wraps a request in a watcher thread that force-kills
  the child process (`taskkill /F /T` on Windows, `kill -9` elsewhere) if the
  deadline passes before the response arrives, surfacing
  `Error::HandshakeTimeout { method, elapsed_ms }` instead of hanging.
- On a non-JSON-RPC handshake response, `Error::InvalidResponse` now includes
  a bounded (200-byte), path-redacted snippet of what was actually received
  (`redact_snippet`), e.g. `got non-JSON-RPC output from Runtime for
  'initialize' (...); first bytes: Simplicio Runtime booting, please wait...`.
- `spawn_in`'s `initialize`/`tools/list` calls now go through
  `request_with_timeout`.

This is diagnostics/UX hardening only — it does not touch or mask the actual
server-side bug tracked at simplicio-runtime#3319.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build -p simplicio-runtime-client --locked`
- [x] `cargo test -p simplicio-runtime-client --locked` — new
      `tests/fake_server_malformed_handshake.rs` drives the fake stdio server
      (`tests/support/fake_server_main.rs`, extended with a
      `SIMPLICIO_FAKE_SERVER_MODE` switch: `banner_then_json` /
      `hang_handshake`) through both failure modes without any real Runtime
      binary:
      - non-JSON-RPC banner in place of the `initialize` reply → fails in
        well under 2s with a diagnostic containing the raw snippet.
      - Runtime that never answers `initialize` → `Error::HandshakeTimeout`
        fires at ~`DEFAULT_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS`, not the old exec timeout.
- [x] Existing `tests/fake_server_search.rs` (search/read/write/delete
      round-trip + fail-closed contract) still passes, unaffected.
- [x] `cargo fmt -p simplicio-runtime-client`

title: harden(gateway): surface Retry-After hints on 429/5xx (issue #4)
body:
Part of #4

## Context

PR #24 added `crates/codegen/simplicio-code-gateway` as a new, additive,
standalone crate (auth state machine + private gateway HTTP client,
24/24 tests passing) but explicitly did **not** wire it into any
production request path. It stayed deliberately unwired: swapping the
real default-model routing away from OpenRouter is large, cross-cutting
surgery through `xai-grok-shell/src/agent/config.rs` (>11k lines, with
hundreds of existing tests asserting current `base_url`/BYOK/credential
behavior), and PR #24's author found and reverted an in-progress,
unverified edit to `crates/codegen/xai-grok-models/default_models.json`
that had started that swap without proof it was safe.

## What I decided to do, and why

I re-surveyed the production path before touching anything:

- `crates/codegen/xai-grok-sampler/src/client.rs` (`SamplingClient`,
  lines ~401-939) is the single, real HTTP client that talks to
  OpenRouter today — headers, auth, streaming SSE, all in one type,
  with no provider enum/dispatch to hook a second client into.
- `crates/codegen/xai-grok-shell/src/agent/config.rs` resolves
  `base_url`/`api_key`/`env_key` per model entry (functions in the
  4332-4670 range) and has a dedicated inline test module plus several
  standalone test files (`tests/common/mod.rs`,
  `team_managed_config.rs`, `test_built_binary_e2e.rs`,
  `test_doom_loop_recovery.rs`, session/ACP memory-config tests, and
  `xai-grok-sampler`'s own ~600-line auth-header test module) that all
  assert current BYOK/`base_url` behavior.
- `crates/codegen/xai-grok-models/default_models.json` — the file a
  previous agent's risky edit already broke once — has one entry
  (`simplicio-1`), no `"provider"` field, and no existing feature-flag
  mechanism to add a second provider behind (the closest precedent,
  `GROK_MANAGED_MCP_GATEWAY_TOOLS_ENABLED`, gates tool exposure, not
  provider routing).

Wiring the gateway crate in as a real, flag-gated, selectable provider
would mean: adding a provider-dispatch layer that doesn't exist yet,
threading it through the config resolution path, and re-verifying the
whole existing BYOK/`base_url` test surface — genuinely valuable, but
not something I can complete *and prove safe* in one session without
risking exactly the kind of unreviewed production change this task
explicitly told me to avoid, and that a prior agent already had to
revert once in this same area. I did not touch
`xai-grok-shell/src/agent/config.rs` or
`xai-grok-models/default_models.json` in this change.

So I took the honest fallback: **harden the standalone gateway crate**,
scoped strictly inside `crates/codegen/simplicio-code-gateway`, zero
production blast radius.

## What changed

`PrivateGateway` (`models()`, `usage()`, `chat_stream()`) silently
discarded the `Retry-After` response header on 429/5xx errors —
confirmed by reading the existing `rate_limited_429_is_a_stable_server_error`
test, which already sent `retry-after: 12` but never asserted anything
about it reaching the caller. Callers had a stable
`GatewayError::Server{status, message}` but no backoff signal, so any
future caller (wired-in or not) would have no way to respect
rate-limit hints without re-parsing raw HTTP responses itself.

- `GatewayError::Server` now carries `retry_after: Option<Duration>`,
  parsed from the `Retry-After` header (delay-seconds form; HTTP-date
  form is intentionally left unparsed — this server doesn't emit it,
  and guessing at clock-skew-sensitive date arithmetic for a form we
  don't expect trades a small feature for a real correctness risk, per
  the new `parse_retry_after` doc comment).
- Wired into both call sites: `decode_json` (used by `models()`/`usage()`)
  and `chat_stream()`'s failure branch.
- Missing/unparseable headers degrade safely to `None`, never a
  fabricated default or a panic.

## Tests (real, run this session, on this machine)

```
cargo test -p simplicio-code-gateway
  8 passed  (src/auth.rs + src/gateway.rs + src/redaction.rs unit tests, 1 ignored)
  6 passed  (tests/contract.rs)
  1 passed  (tests/fake_contract.rs)
  16 passed (tests/wiremock_contract.rs, real HTTP against wiremock)
total: 31 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored (OsSecretStore real-keychain test, same as PR #24)

cargo clippy -p simplicio-code-gateway --all-targets -- -D warnings
  clean, no warnings
```

New/updated tests in `tests/wiremock_contract.rs`:
- `rate_limited_429_is_a_stable_server_error` — updated to assert
  `retry_after == Some(Duration::from_secs(12))` (previously asserted
  nothing about the header it was already sending).
- `rate_limited_429_without_retry_after_header_yields_no_hint` (new)
- `rate_limited_429_with_unparseable_retry_after_yields_no_hint` (new)
  — HTTP-date header must degrade to `None`, not panic or misparse.
- `chat_stream_429_surfaces_retry_after_before_failing` (new) — same
  guarantee on the streaming POST failure path, not just the GET/JSON
  path.
- `unauthorized_401_is_a_stable_server_error_without_leaking_the_body`
  — updated for the new field, asserts `retry_after == None` on a
  response with no such header.

`cargo fmt --all -- --check` reports pre-existing diffs in
`crates/codegen/simplicio-code-gateway/src/auth.rs` and
`benches/gateway_contract.rs` — files this change does **not** touch;
that non-standard compact style predates this PR (from #24). Not fixed
here to keep this diff scoped to the actual change.

## Honest list of what's still not wired into production

- The gateway crate is still **not** reachable from any real request
  path — no config flag, no provider enum, no code in
  `xai-grok-shell`/`xai-grok-sampler` references it. `default_models.json`
  is unchanged.
- `GatewayLimits::enforce` remains a client-side pre-check only; there's
  still no server to enforce quotas against.
- macOS/Linux `OsSecretStore` keychain round-trips remain unverified by
  me (Windows-only sandbox, same limitation as #24).
- No automatic retry/backoff loop was added — this change only
  *surfaces* the hint; auto-retrying non-idempotent chat requests
  without a dedup/idempotency-key mechanism felt like a second,
  separate risk not to bundle in here.

title: privacy(#13): network destination inventory + telemetry allowlist + capture test
body:
Part of #13.

PR #27 already delivered `DO_NOT_TRACK` opt-out and `simplicio-code privacy diagnose`. This closes the remaining acceptance criteria from the issue as far as is realistically achievable in one client-side slice: a full network-destination inventory, a schema allowlist, and a real test that captures outbound traffic for the telemetry paths.

## What's here

- **`docs/privacy/network-destinations.md`** — a full, file:line-cited inventory of every network destination the client can contact, both core-product (inference, auth, session sync, self-update, plugin marketplace, asset CDN) and telemetry/analytics/crash-reporting. Built by reading every crate that hardcodes a URL or constructs an HTTP/WebSocket client: `xai-grok-telemetry`, `xai-mixpanel`, `xai-crash-handler`, `xai-grok-env`, `xai-grok-shell` (agent/config, remote/*, session/storage, upload/gcs), `xai-grok-update`, `xai-grok-plugin-marketplace`, `xai-grok-agent`, `xai-grok-sampler`, `xai-grok-tools`, `xai-grok-config-types`, `simplicio-account-client`. Each entry has a file:line citation and a DO_NOT_TRACK-coverage column.
- **`xai-grok-telemetry::allowlist`** — `ALLOWED_TELEMETRY_HOSTS = ["api.mixpanel.com", "cli-chat-proxy.grok.com"]` and a fail-closed `assert_allowed_telemetry_url()` (unparsable URL / no host / non-allowlisted host are all rejected; exact-string host match only, so lookalike/typosquat hosts don't pass). Scoped honestly to the telemetry surface this crate owns — Sentry's DSN and customer-configured external OTEL are documented exceptions (dynamic/customer-supplied host), not silently-ignored gaps.
- **`xai-mixpanel::Mixpanel::with_client_and_base_url`** — lets the Mixpanel client be redirected away from `api.mixpanel.com` (new `TelemetryConfig.mixpanel_base_url` / `GROK_TELEMETRY_MIXPANEL_BASE_URL` override), mirroring the existing `events_url` override. This is what makes the capture test possible without touching the real Mixpanel endpoint.
- **`tests/network_destination_allowlist.rs`** — a real network-capture test, modeled on the already-merged `tests/manual_auth_emit.rs` pattern: spins up an actual local HTTP server on loopback, redirects both telemetry sinks this crate owns (product-events `events_url` + Mixpanel `mixpanel_base_url`) at it, drives a representative operation (`client::init` + `log_event(ManualAuth)`, which together fire `/events`, `/track`, and `/engage`), and asserts via a catch-all fallback route that **nothing else ever lands on that server**. A second unit test cross-checks that the production defaults (no override) resolve to hosts in `ALLOWED_TELEMETRY_HOSTS`.

## Test evidence

```
cargo test -p xai-mixpanel --lib
running 3 tests
test tests::default_constructors_use_production_base_url ... ok
test tests::override_constructor_replaces_base_url ... ok
test tests::prepare_properties_scrubs_then_injects_token ... ok
test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.03s
```

**Honest gap**: `cargo test -p xai-grok-telemetry` (which would exercise the new `allowlist.rs` unit tests and `network_destination_allowlist.rs`) could not be run in this Windows sandbox — `xai-grok-tools-api`'s build script fails on the bundled `protoc` binary (`%1 não é um aplicativo Win32 válido`, i.e. wrong-architecture binary), a pre-existing environment problem unrelated to this change (same category of Windows build blocker PR #27 already flagged for `xai-proto-build`). The new tests are written directly against the same pattern as `tests/manual_auth_emit.rs` (an existing, already-merged, already-passing test doing the identical local-axum-server capture), but I could not execute them myself in this session. Flagging this rather than claiming false confidence.

## What remains open (honestly, per the original #13 acceptance criteria)

- **No full-process MITM/proxy capture.** This proves the two telemetry sinks this crate owns only contact their configured host for a representative operation. It does not spawn the built binary and intercept every socket the whole process opens — that would also have to account for the core-product destinations in the inventory's first table, which are *expected* to fire (inference, auth, sync). That needs a system-level proxy harness and CI plumbing beyond a single crate's test suite; tracked as follow-up.
- **Sentry and external-OTEL hosts are not in the machine-checked allowlist** because their host isn't a fixed compile-time string (build/env-supplied DSN, customer-configured endpoint respectively). Documented as a deliberate limitation in the allowlist module doc comment, not an oversight.
- Backend retention/deletion enforcement and Mixpanel removal remain open exactly as PR #27 already documented (unchanged by this PR).

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo test -p xai-mixpanel --lib` — 3 passed, 0 failed
- [ ] `cargo test -p xai-grok-telemetry` — blocked by pre-existing Windows `protoc` build issue in this sandbox, not run
- [x] `cargo fmt -p xai-mixpanel -p xai-grok-telemetry -- --check` — clean on every line this PR touches (pre-existing formatting drift elsewhere in those files, from an apparent rustfmt version difference, is left untouched/out of scope)
